about.heading: 关于亩每小时作业速率计算器
about.body:
亩每小时作业速率计算器是一款实用的农业生产力工具，帮助农民、承包商和农艺师衡量田间设备覆盖土地的效率。了解作业速率对于安排作业计划、估算燃料和人工成本、比较不同机器的性能，以及为代耕或定制作业报价都至关重要。无论你在北美把它称为 acres per hour（ac/hr），还是在使用公制的国家称为 hectares per hour（ha/hr），其核心概念都是一样的：你的机器在给定时间内能覆盖多少面积？

该计算器提供两种互补的方法。第一种——面积与时间——最简单也最直接。你只需测量或知道已完成的总面积，以及实际耗时。用面积除以时间即可得到有效作业速率，这会自动包含作业过程中发生的所有真实延误，例如地头转弯、加料停顿和故障。若你需要的是实际田间平均值而不是理论最大值，这就是最佳方法。

第二种方法——机器参数——允许你在田间作业开始前，根据设备参数估算理论作业速率。对于英制设备，公式为：作业速率（ac/hr）= 宽度（ft）× 速度（mph）× 效率（%）÷ 8.25。常数 8.25 用于将英尺与英里/小时的乘积换算为每小时英亩数。对于公制设备，公式为：作业速率（ha/hr）= 宽度（m）× 速度（km/h）× 效率（%）÷ 10。效率系数——多数田间作业通常为 75–90%——用于反映转弯、重叠和短暂停机带来的真实损失，而无需逐项记录每次作业。

田间效率是机器参数计算中影响最大的变量之一。仅凭割台宽度和速度，联合收割机的理论能力可能达到 20 ac/hr，但若效率系数为 80%，实际作业能力就只有 16 ac/hr。理解这一差距有助于操作人员设定更现实的日作业目标。常见效率范围包括：播种机和条播机 75–80%，喷雾机 70–80%，联合收割机 65–80%，整地设备 80–90%。

作业速率数据可直接用于成本分析。如果你知道机器每小时的总运行成本——燃料、折旧、人工和维修合计——就可以用它除以作业速率，得到每英亩或每公顷成本。一台以 12 ac/hr 运行、总成本为 96 美元/小时的机器，其成本就是每英亩 8 美元。把两台机器并排比较，就能判断更宽、更快但小时成本更高的机型，是否仍能带来更低的单位面积成本。这是任何扩张型经营中经典的资本投入决策。

该计算器同时支持英亩和公顷，因此在北美、欧洲、澳大利亚和南美的农业体系中都同样实用。美国和加拿大可使用英制模式进行 ASABE 标准规划；欧洲 CAP 补贴文件、默认以公顷为单位的精准农业软件，或任何已经使用 SI 单位的产量图和田间报告项目，则适合使用公制模式。

howto.heading: 如何使用亩每小时作业速率计算器
steps: 选择计算模式：如果你已经知道田块面积和耗时，就选“面积与时间”；如果想根据设备参数估算，就选“机器参数”。
在面积与时间模式下：输入田块面积并选择单位（英亩或公顷），再输入耗时（小时），然后点击“计算作业速率”。
在机器参数模式下：先选择英制或公制，然后输入机器工作宽度、行驶速度和田间效率百分比（默认 85%），再点击“计算作业速率”。
在结果面板中查看作业速率（ac/hr 或 ha/hr）。结果下方会显示所用公式，便于核对。
点击“重置数值”即可清空所有输入，并用不同参数重新开始计算。

faq.heading: 亩每小时作业速率计算器常见问题

items.q: 什么是田间作业速率，为什么它很重要？
items.a: 田间作业速率是指机器单位时间内覆盖的面积，通常以每小时英亩数或每小时公顷数表示。它很重要，因为它决定了一项作业需要多少天、会消耗多少燃料和人工，以及最终每英亩的作业成本是多少。准确的作业速率数据是所有农场成本与排程模型的基础。

items.q: 我应该使用什么田间效率值？
items.a: 田间效率用于反映地头转弯、补料、轻微调整和短暂停机所损失的时间。播种机和条播机通常为 75–80%，喷雾机为 70–80%，联合收割机为 65–80%，整地机具为 80–90%。如果想要最准确的结果，最好使用你自己经营中的历史记录；如果没有数据，可先用 80% 作为保守的通用估计。

items.q: 为什么英制公式要用 8.25 作为除数？
items.a: 常数 8.25 用于把英尺（宽度）和英里/小时（速度）的乘积换算成每小时英亩数。1 英亩等于 43,560 平方英尺，1 英里等于 5,280 英尺，因此 width_ft × speed_mph 会得到 ft²/hr × 5280。再除以 43,560，就可简化为除以 8.25（即 43,560 / 5,280）。这是北美农业工程中通用的 ASABE 换算常数。

items.q: 对应的公制公式是什么？
items.a: 对于公制机器，作业速率（ha/hr）= 宽度（m）× 速度（km/h）× 效率（%）÷ 10。除数 10 来自 1 公顷 = 10,000 m² 和 1 km = 1,000 m 的关系，因此 m × km/h 得到 m²·km/hr，除以 10 后即可得到 ha/hr。两个公式在量纲上是等价的，只是单位不同。

items.q: 如何把每小时英亩数换算成每小时公顷数？
items.a: 将每小时英亩数乘以 0.40468564224 即可得到每小时公顷数。例如，10 ac/hr × 0.40468564224 = 4.047 ha/hr。反过来，将 ha/hr 乘以 2.4710538147 可换回 ac/hr。该计算器原生支持两套单位系统，因此你无需手动换算。

items.q: 这个计算器可以用于非农业场景吗？
items.a: 可以。只要是移动设备按已知速度覆盖某个面积的任务，这个公式都适用——例如草坪修剪、道路清扫、推雪、用滚筒涂刷表面，或机器人吸尘覆盖面积。输入工具宽度、行驶速度以及适合任务的效率系数，计算器就会给出每小时覆盖面积。

about.heading: 关于等位基因频率计算器
about.body:
等位基因频率计算器是一款群体遗传学工具，用于计算描述群体内遗传变异的核心指标：每个等位基因的频率、每种基因型的频率，以及该群体是否处于 Hardy-Weinberg 平衡（HWE）。这些计算在遗传学和进化生物学课程、临床遗传学、保护生物学，以及任何涉及群体水平 DNA 数据的研究中都非常重要。

等位基因频率是群体遗传学中最基础、也最重要的汇总指标。对于双等位基因系统——显性等位基因 A 和隐性等位基因 a——等位基因频率记为 p（A 的频率）和 q（a 的频率），并满足 p + q = 1。从基因型计数出发，p = (2 × AA + Aa) / (2 × N)，其中 N 是二倍体个体总数。分母 2N 代表群体中的等位基因总数，因为每个二倍体个体携带两个拷贝。若直接使用等位基因计数，则 p = A / (A + a)，q = a / (A + a)。

基因型频率描述群体中三类二倍体基因型各自所占的比例：AA（纯合显性）、Aa（杂合）和 aa（纯合隐性）。观察到的基因型频率就是相应计数除以 N。Hardy-Weinberg 平衡下的期望基因型频率分别为 p²、2pq 和 q²，这是 Hardy-Weinberg 原理的预测结果。

Hardy-Weinberg 原理指出，在一个足够大、随机交配、没有突变、迁移、自然选择或遗传漂变的群体中，等位基因和基因型频率会世代保持不变。HWE 下的期望基因型计数分别是 AA 的 p² × N、Aa 的 2pq × N，以及 aa 的 q² × N。当观察到的基因型计数与这些期望值存在显著差异时，说明该群体不处于 HWE，这可能提示近交、群体分层、作用于该位点的自然选择，或实验数据中的分型错误。

卡方检验为判断偏离 HWE 的程度是否大于随机波动所能解释的范围提供了正式的统计框架。检验统计量等于三个基因型类别中 (观察值 − 期望值)² / 期望值 的总和。以 1 个自由度（2 个等位基因、3 个基因型、1 个约束）计算，在 p = 0.05 时的临界值为 3.841。卡方值低于 3.841 说明与 HWE 相符；高于该值则表示存在统计学显著偏离。

它的实际应用非常广泛。在临床遗传学中，对 SNP 进行 HWE 检验是标准质控步骤——对照组分型数据中系统性的 HWE 偏离通常提示批次效应或分型错误。在保护生物学中，小而破碎的种群若偏离 HWE，可能意味着近交衰退或遗传瓶颈。在法医遗传学中，HWE 假设是计算 DNA 画像随机匹配概率的乘法规则基础。了解疾病相关等位基因的 p 和 q，也能帮助流行病学家使用 2pq 公式估算携带者频率。

howto.heading: 如何使用等位基因频率计算器
steps: 选择输入方式：如果你有 AA、Aa 和 aa 的计数，请选“基因型计数”；如果你有每种等位基因的直接计数，请选“等位基因计数”。
基因型计数模式：输入纯合显性（AA）、杂合（Aa）和纯合隐性（aa）个体的数量。
等位基因计数模式：输入 A 等位基因和 a 等位基因的数量。也可以输入总个体数，以便得到按比例缩放的 HWE 期望计数。
点击“计算”。工具会显示 p 和 q、总 N、HWE 下的期望基因型计数，以及（在基因型输入模式下）用于判断 HWE 偏离的卡方检验。
点击“重置”即可清空所有字段并开始新的计算。

faq.heading: 等位基因频率计算器常见问题

items.q: 在遗传学中，p 和 q 代表什么？
items.a: 在双等位基因系统中，p 表示显性等位基因（A）的频率，q 表示隐性等位基因（a）的频率。它们始终满足 p + q = 1，因为该位点上的每个等位基因都只能是 A 或 a。等位基因频率范围从 0（该等位基因不存在）到 1（该等位基因在群体中固定）。

items.q: 什么是 Hardy-Weinberg 平衡？
items.a: Hardy-Weinberg 平衡（HWE）描述的是一种理论状态：群体中的等位基因和基因型频率在世代之间保持不变。它需要五个条件：群体足够大、随机交配、无突变、无迁移、无自然选择。在 HWE 下，期望基因型频率分别为 p²（AA）、2pq（Aa）和 q²（aa）。

items.q: 为什么我的群体可能偏离 HWE？
items.a: HWE 偏离的常见原因包括近交（降低杂合度）、群体分层（遗传上不同的亚群混合）、该位点受到强烈自然选择、近期迁移，或实验数据中的分型错误。纯合子过量通常提示近交；杂合子过量可能意味着群体分层或杂合子优势。

items.q: 如何解读 HWE 的卡方检验？
items.a: 卡方统计量衡量观察到的基因型计数与期望值之间的差异。自由度为 1 时，若数值低于 3.841，则在 5% 显著性水平下不显著（与 HWE 相符）。若高于 3.841，则说明显著偏离 HWE。注意，当样本量非常小（N < 20）时，卡方近似并不可靠，通常应优先使用精确检验。

items.q: 这个计算器可以用于 X 连锁位点吗？
items.a: 这里展示的标准 Hardy-Weinberg 公式适用于常染色体位点。对于 X 连锁位点，男性是半合子（只携带一个等位基因），因此男性中隐性表型的频率等于 q，而不是 q²。X 连锁位点需要单独的 HWE 分析，不过从女性基因型计数计算等位基因频率（p 和 q）仍然使用同样的公式。

items.q: 常见疾病变异的典型等位基因频率是多少？
items.a: 全基因组关联研究（GWAS）中识别的常见疾病相关 SNP，次要等位基因频率（MAF）通常在 0.05 到 0.50 之间。罕见孟德尔病变异的频率可能远低于 0.01（1%）。隐性疾病的携带者频率等于 2pq，因此对于 q = 0.01 的隐性等位基因，携带者频率约为 2 × 0.99 × 0.01 ≈ 2%。

about.heading: 关于动物死亡率计算器
about.body:
动物死亡率计算器用于计算在给定时间段内，某一明确群体中死亡动物所占的百分比。它是兽医学、畜牧业、家禽生产、野生动物管理和生态研究中的基础指标。监测死亡率有助于生产者和科学家及早发现疾病暴发，评估健康干预措施的效果，将群体或禽群表现与行业标准进行比较，并满足监管和动物福利报告义务。

计算本身很简单：死亡率（%）=（死亡数 ÷ 总数量）× 100。其对应值存活率（%）= 100 − 死亡率，表示在该期间存活下来的比例。与只看死亡率相比，同时了解这两个数值——以及实际存活数量——在实践中更有用，因为它可以直接回答管理问题：我还剩多少动物？又损失了多少？

可选的上下文字段——期间、年龄组、死亡原因和备注——可以把单个数字转化为结构化的死亡记录。期间将测量锚定到一个日历区间（周、生产周期、季节或年份），这对于趋势分析和不同长度期间的比较至关重要。年龄组可帮助你区分新生期死亡率和成体死亡率，而在围产期损失占总死亡中很大比例的畜牧系统中，这种区分尤为重要。死亡原因可将该比率与具体病因或状况关联起来——传染病、外伤、营养缺乏、环境应激——从而实现更精准的控制措施。

不同物种和生产系统的基准死亡率差异很大。在商业肉鸡生产中，6周育成期的可接受累计死亡率通常为 3–5%。蛋鸡群的月死亡率应低于 0.5%。奶牛在良好管理下的年死亡率大约为 3–6%。从出生到上市的猪场通常会损失 5–8% 的猪，其中最高死亡率出现在生命第一周。研究有蹄类种群的野生动物生物学家认为，在恶劣年份，10–25% 的冬季死亡率属正常，而超过 30% 则提示存在种群层面的应激因素。将你计算出的比率与这些基准进行比较，可立即判断观察到的死亡水平是否需要干预。

从流行病学角度看，死亡率与病死率（CFR）和死亡风险密切相关。CFR 指被诊断患有某种特定疾病的动物中，死于该病的比例；而一般死亡率则统计所有原因导致的死亡，不论诊断结果如何。这两个指标都被追踪于 USDA-APHIS 报告和 OIE（世界动物卫生组织）数据库等疾病监测系统中。理解二者差异有助于生产者和兽医更准确地交流动物健康数据，并就疫苗接种计划、生物安全投入和淘汰策略作出有依据的决策。

howto.heading: 如何使用动物死亡率计算器
steps: 在“总数量”字段中输入观察期开始时的动物总数。
在“死亡数”字段中输入期间内发生的死亡数量。死亡数必须介于 0 和总数量之间。
可选填写期间、年龄组、死亡原因和备注字段，为记录或报告生成完整的死亡记录。

faq.heading: 动物死亡率计算器常见问题

items.q: 畜禽的正常死亡率是多少？
items.a: 可接受范围因物种和生产系统而异。商业肉鸡在 6 周育成期通常为 3–5%。奶牛的全年全因死亡率约为 3–6%。从出生到上市的猪场通常会损失 5–8%。蛋鸡每月应低于 0.5%。任何持续高于这些基准的数值都值得兽医调查。

items.q: 死亡率与病死率有什么区别？
items.a: 死亡率是指在某一时期内，总群体中因所有原因死亡所占的比例，不论是否患病。病死率（CFR）是指被诊断患有某种特定疾病的动物中，死于该病的比例。死亡率是群体层面的指标；CFR 是疾病严重程度指标。两者都很重要，但回答的问题不同。

items.q: 死亡数里要不要包括淘汰动物？
items.a: 这取决于你的报告目的。对于福利和疾病监测，因人道原因进行的淘汰（重病、重伤）通常计入死亡数，因为它们代表从生产群体中损失的动物。出于生产原因的计划性淘汰（如产蛋周期结束、达到上市体重）通常单独作为处置而非死亡报告。请遵循你所在国家的动物健康报告标准。

items.q: 如何比较不同长度期间的死亡率？
items.a: 将每个比率换算为相同时间单位，通常为年化死亡率。例如，如果 30 天内死亡率为 2%，年化率约为 2% × (365/30) ≈ 24%。或者使用日死亡率（每只动物每天的死亡数），再乘以期间长度。报告死亡率时务必注明期间，以确保比较有意义。

items.q: 什么样的死亡率会触发疾病调查？
items.a: 任何高于该物种和生产系统基线的突然上升都应触发调查。经验上，如果在短时间内（几天到一周）出现超过正常水平 2–3 个百分点且无法解释的上升，就应立即进行兽医评估。一些监管阈值是绝对值：例如，许多国家要求在家禽日死亡率超过 0.5% 时上报，因为这种模式可能提示高致病性禽流感。

items.q: 这个计算器能用于鱼类或水产养殖吗？
items.a: 可以。相同公式——死亡率 =（死亡数 / 总数量）× 100——适用于任何动物群体，包括鱼、虾和其他水产养殖物种。在水产养殖中，日死亡率尤其重要，因为养殖密度高，损失累积很快。鲑鱼养殖中可接受的典型日死亡率低于 0.1%；更高的数值通常意味着疾病、缺氧或操作应激。

about.heading: 关于退火温度计算器
about.body:
退火温度计算器会根据一对寡核苷酸引物的DNA序列和反应条件，预测最佳PCR退火温度（Ta）。正确设定退火温度是PCR实验设计中最重要的决定之一：温度过低会出现非特异性产物，温度过高则引物无法高效结合，导致产量下降甚至完全无法扩增。这个计算器通过应用成熟的热力学模型，帮助你在操作热循环仪之前得到可靠的起始值。

引物的熔解温度（Tm）是指一半引物-模板双链解离成单链时的温度。退火温度通常设为比引物对中较低的Tm低5°C，因此公式为：Ta = min(Tm_forward, Tm_reverse) − 5°C。这个经验法则在特异性结合与延伸效率之间取得平衡，也是全球大多数分子生物学实验室采用的标准。

该计算器提供三种Tm预测方法，它们在准确度和适用性上各不相同。Wallace规则（Tm = 2°C × (A+T) + 4°C × (G+C)）是最简单、最快速的估算方法。它对长度约20个碱基以内的引物相当准确，适合快速做一次合理性检查，但它忽略了最近邻堆叠相互作用，也没有盐校正。需要快速粗略估算时可使用它。

GC含量方法（Tm = 69.3 + 41 × GC_fraction − 650/n）是针对短寡核苷酸调整过的 Marmur-Doty-Schildkraut 公式。它同时考虑引物长度（n）和GC比例，因此在常见的18–30个碱基引物范围内，比Wallace规则更准确。由于它把同类型碱基对视为热力学等效，因此仍然只是近似值。

最近邻方法（SantaLucia 1998）是最准确的方案，也是 Primer3 和 OligoCalc 等商业引物设计工具所采用的方法。它通过累加引物上每个相邻二核苷酸对的焓和熵贡献来计算 ΔH 和 ΔS，然后使用热力学公式 Tm = ΔH / (ΔS + R × ln(CT)) − 273.15，其中 R 为气体常数，CT 为总链浓度。随后再用公式 Tm_corrected = Tm + 16.6 × log10([Na+]/1000) 进行盐校正。这种方法是适用于任何长度和GC含量引物的金标准，尤其适合明显偏离平均组成的高GC或高AT序列。

盐浓度很重要，因为单价阳离子会通过屏蔽带负电的磷酸骨架之间的排斥作用来稳定DNA双螺旋。盐浓度越高，Tm越高；盐浓度越低，Tm越低。默认的50 mM Na⁺ 适合标准PCR缓冲液。DNA（引物）浓度会通过最近邻公式中的 ln(CT) 项影响 Tm；默认的250 nM 适用于典型PCR反应。根据你的实际反应条件调整这些数值，可以提高预测准确性。

examples.intro: 不同GC组成和长度范围的示例引物，展示三种计算方法下的结果。

items.note: 标准基因扩增引物。最近邻方法。盐浓度50 mM，引物浓度250 nM。

howto.heading: 如何使用退火温度计算器
steps: 在第一个输入框中输入正向引物的DNA序列（5’ 到 3’ 方向）。仅使用 ATGC 碱基；歧义碱基和空格会被忽略。
可选输入反向引物序列。如果提供，Ta 将基于一对匹配引物中较低的两个Tm值来计算。
设置盐浓度（默认 50 mM NaCl）和 DNA/引物浓度（默认 250 nM）。使用你的实际反应条件可获得最佳准确度。
选择计算方法：Wallace规则用于快速粗略估算，GC含量用于中等准确度，最近邻（SantaLucia 1998）用于最准确的结果。
点击计算温度。结果面板将显示推荐的退火温度（Ta）以及每条引物的Tm值和碱基组成统计。

faq.heading: 退火温度计算器常见问题

items.q: Tm 和 Ta 有什么区别？
items.a: Tm（熔解温度）是指50%的引物-模板双链解离成单链时的温度，它是引物本身和反应条件共同决定的性质。Ta（退火温度）是PCR热循环仪在退火步骤中使用的温度。Ta通常设为比引物对中较低的Tm低5°C，以确保引物高效且特异地结合。

items.q: 我应该使用哪种计算方法？
items.a: 对于大多数实验室PCR工作，最近邻（SantaLucia 1998）方法最准确，也是 NCBI Primer-BLAST 和 Primer3 推荐的方法。Wallace规则只适合非常短的引物（少于14个碱基）或快速心算检查。GC含量方法适合在需要比Wallace更准确、但又比最近邻更快的场景，例如初步筛选引物时。

items.q: 为什么即使使用计算出的退火温度，我的PCR还是失败？
items.a: 计算出的Ta只是起点，不是保证。真实的PCR表现还取决于引物特异性（用BLAST检查）、引物二聚体和发卡结构（用 OligoAnalyzer 检查）、模板二级结构、Mg2⁺ 浓度、聚合酶过程性以及热循环仪升降温速率。如果计算温度不起作用，尝试在 Ta 上下 ±2–10°C 进行梯度PCR，或者每次以 2°C 为步进降低退火温度。

items.q: 盐浓度会如何影响退火温度？
items.a: 较高的单价盐（Na⁺、K⁺）浓度会通过屏蔽带负电的磷酸骨架来稳定DNA双螺旋，从而提高Tm。将NaCl从25 mM提高到100 mM，通常会使Tm上升2–3°C。标准PCR缓冲液含有50–75 mM KCl以及 Mg2⁺；若要在最近邻盐校正中获得最佳结果，请输入 Na⁺ 等效浓度。

items.q: 如果一对引物的Tm差异很大，我该怎么办？
items.a: 当一对引物的Tm差异超过5°C时，建议重新设计较低Tm的引物，使其更长或GC含量更高，以缩小差异。较大的Tm不匹配会导致在选定的Ta下，其中一个引物结合效率较低，从而降低产量并促进单侧产物生成。作为折中方案，有时可以使用降落PCR程序：先从高于较高Tm的温度开始，再逐步跨越退火温度范围下降。

items.q: 在DMSO或其他添加剂存在时，引物Tm会变化吗？
items.a: 会。DMSO会削弱双螺旋稳定性，使Tm每增加1% DMSO大约下降0.5–1.0°C。甜菜碱、甘油和甲酰胺也会类似地降低Tm。这些添加剂常用于扩增高GC或结构复杂的模板；在它们存在时，应将Ta相应降低大致相同的幅度。该计算器不模拟添加剂效应，因此如果你的反应包含共溶剂，需要手动调整Ta。

result.heading: 胸高断面积结果
result.formula: 公式：BA = π × (DBH/2)²。厘米制：BA (m²) = π × (DBH/200)²。英寸制：BA (ft²) = π × (DBH/24)²。

about.heading: 关于树木胸高断面积计算器
about.body:
胸高断面积计算器用于计算树干在胸高处（离地 1.3 m 或 4.5 ft）横截面的面积，这也是测量胸高直径（DBH）的标准高度。胸高断面积（BA）是林业和生态学中最常用的指标之一，因为它能把林分中树木的大小与数量整合成一个数值，并且与林分生物量、材积、光拦截和竞争关系高度相关。

单株树木的公式是：BA = π × (DBH/2)²。若采用公制且 DBH 以厘米计，则公式变为 BA (m²) = π × (DBH_cm / 200)²，因为直径除以 2 得到半径，再除以 100 将厘米换算为米。若采用英制且 DBH 以英寸计，则 BA (ft²) = π × (DBH_in / 24)²，逻辑在英尺单位下相同。对于样地或林分，总胸高断面积等于各单木 BA 之和。

单位面积胸高断面积——以 m²/ha 或 ft²/acre 表示——是森林清查、采伐规划和营林措施中使用的林分层级指标。计算方法是将样地总 BA 除以样地面积（用相同单位），再换算到参考面积（1 公顷 = 10,000 m²；1 英亩 = 43,560 ft²）。在输入样地面积后，计算器会自动完成这种换算。常见数值从开阔稀树草原的 5 m²/ha 到致密温带雨林的 50+ m²/ha 不等。大多数经营性用材林通常维持在 15–35 m²/ha，具体取决于树种和轮伐目标。

林业工作者将 BA/ha 作为间伐处方的主要输入。间伐后的目标 BA（称为残留 BA）由各树种和立地条件对应的产量表或生长模型确定。如果实测 BA 高于目标值，说明林分郁闭过度，建议间伐；如果 BA 低于目标值，则可以保持不扰动，或考虑补植增密。

生态学家还在另外两个重要场景中使用胸高断面积。第一，相对胸高断面积——某一树种的 BA 占总林分 BA 的比例——是植被调查和样地研究中衡量物种优势度的标准指标。第二，林分胸高断面积与冠层郁闭度以及光合有效辐射的拦截密切相关，因此可用于预测林下光照可用性，以及耐阴地表层植物的生境条件。

胸径实测小技巧：使用直径尺带（D-tape）可提高精度；在坡地上应从上坡侧量到精确 1.3 m；多干树需分别测量各主干并汇总单木 BA；倾斜树应沿与树干轴线垂直的方向测量。与绝对精度相比，测量流程的一致性更重要，因为林分之间及不同时期的相对比较才是大多数经营决策的依据。

examples.heading: 胸高断面积计算示例
examples.intro: 包含公制和英制，以及有无样地面积的单树和多树场景。

500 m² 样地中的 3 棵树。BA/ha = 0.1963 / 500 × 10,000 = 39.3 m²/ha —— 属于中等郁闭度的温带森林。

howto.heading: 如何使用胸高断面积计算器
steps: 在第一个输入框中输入一个或多个胸径值，用逗号分隔（例如“25, 30, 40”）。如果只有一棵树，只输入一个数值。
选择胸径单位（厘米或英寸）以及所需的胸高断面积输出单位（m² 或 ft²）。
如需林分级结果，可选填写样地面积并选择其单位，以计算每公顷或每英亩的胸高断面积。
点击“计算”。结果面板会显示总 BA；如果你填写了样地面积，还会显示每公顷 BA。
当输入多个胸径值时，点击“显示 / 隐藏步骤”即可查看每棵树的明细表。

faq.heading: 胸高断面积计算器常见问题

items.q: 什么是胸高断面积，为什么林业中要使用它？
items.a: 胸高断面积是指在胸高处（离地 1.3 m 或 4.5 ft）测得的树干横截面积。林业之所以使用它，是因为它能用一个数值高效概括林分密度和蓄积，并且与材积、生物量以及光竞争密切相关。BA/ha 是全球森林清查和间伐处方中的核心指标。

items.q: 经营林分的每公顷胸高断面积通常是多少？
items.a: 数值会因森林类型而差异很大。开阔稀树草原和林地通常为 5–10 m²/ha。典型的温带经营用材林通过定期间伐一般维持在 15–35 m²/ha。未经经营的致密森林可超过 50 m²/ha。热带雨林通常达到 25–40 m²/ha。每个树种的营林指南都会针对特定轮伐期和立地生产力等级给出目标 BA 范围。

items.q: 为什么 DBH 要在离地 1.3 米处测量？
items.a: 胸高 1.3 m（北美为 4.5 ft）的标准之所以被采用，是因为它对使用直径尺带的林业调查人员来说是个方便的工作高度，同时又高于会扭曲低位测量的根膨大和板根，也能确保全球森林清查数据具有可比性。一些热带林业规程会将测量高度调整到 1.5 m 或更高，以避开雨林树木的大板根。

items.q: 我在野外如何准确测量 DBH？
items.a: 使用直径尺带（D-tape）在地面平坦处沿树干 1.3 m 处缠绕测量；在坡地上则从上坡侧测量。D-tape 会直接根据周长读出直径。对于有板根的大树，应在板根以上测量并记录修正后的高度。多干树应分别测量每个主干并汇总胸高断面积。避免在枝杈交汇处或异常膨大处测量。

items.q: 胸高断面积和冠层覆盖度有什么区别？
items.a: 胸高断面积是在固定高度（1.3 m）测得的，反映的是树干横截面积，并与林分材积和蓄积密切相关。冠层覆盖度则是从上方看时，树冠遮蔽地面的比例。两者都是林分密度指标，但衡量的对象不同：BA 是树干的结构性指标，而冠层覆盖度反映的是光拦截。高大、冠幅较窄的针叶林可以具有较高 BA，但冠层覆盖度中等。

items.q: 可以用胸高断面积估算材积吗？
items.a: 可以，胸高断面积是材积方程的重要输入。最简单的形式是用 BA 乘以平均高度和形数：V = BA × H × f，其中 H 为平均林分高度，f 为树种相关的形数（大多数针叶树和阔叶树通常为 0.4–0.6）。更精确的材积表会把 BA 与地位指数和密度指数一起使用。许多国家森林清查系统都会为主要商品树种发布材积-BA 回归方程。

ph-calculator.title: pH计算器 - 酸、碱与缓冲液
ph-calculator.tagline: 使用 Henderson-Hasselbalch 方程，计算强酸、弱酸、强碱、弱碱以及缓冲液的 pH、pOH、[H+] 和 [OH−]。
ph-calculator.intro: 选择溶液类型，输入浓度和所需的解离常数，即可立即得到 pH、pOH 和离子浓度。
ph-calculator.formula: 公式：pH = −log₁₀[H⁺]  •  弱酸 [H⁺] = √(Ka·C)  •  缓冲液 pH = pKa + log₁₀([A⁻]/[HA])  •  pH + pOH = 14

error.invalid: 请输入正的浓度，并为所选溶液类型输入所需常数。

about.heading: 关于 pH 计算器
about.body:
pH 是衡量水溶液酸性或碱性的标准指标。它定义为氢离子浓度的 10 为底负对数：pH = −log₁₀[H⁺]。这种表示法把跨度极大的浓度压缩到一个便于使用的刻度上，通常范围为 0 到 14。pH 为 7 表示中性，低于 7 表示酸性，高于 7 表示碱性。由于这个刻度是对数刻度，每相差 1 个整数，氢离子浓度就相差 10 倍——pH 3 的溶液比 pH 4 酸 10 倍，比 pH 5 酸 100 倍。

这个 pH 计算器可处理普通和分析化学中最常见的五种溶液类型。对于完全解离的强酸，氢离子浓度等于酸的浓度，因此 pH = −log₁₀(C)。对于强碱，氢氧根浓度等于碱的浓度，因此 pOH = −log₁₀(C)，pH = 14 − pOH。这两种情况只需要输入以摩尔每升表示的浓度。

弱酸和弱碱只会部分电离，因此需要其解离常数。对于弱酸，计算器使用平衡近似 [H⁺] = √(Ka · C)，其中 Ka = 10^(−pKa)；对于弱碱，则使用 [OH⁻] = √(Kb · C)。输入 pKa 或 pKb 以及浓度后，pH 计算器会根据得到的离子浓度返回 pH。这个平方根近似假设发生解离的酸或碱的量相对于初始浓度很小，这对典型的稀薄、弱电离溶液通常成立。

缓冲液——由弱酸及其共轭碱组成的混合物——能够抵抗 pH 变化，可用 Henderson-Hasselbalch 方程计算：pH = pKa + log₁₀([A⁻]/[HA])。输入酸的 pKa 以及酸（HA）和其共轭碱（A⁻）的摩尔浓度；当两者相等时，pH 就等于 pKa，这也是缓冲液在接近 pKa 时效果最好的原因。缓冲液在生物化学、细胞培养、发酵以及任何需要保持稳定 pH 的过程中都很重要。

除了 pH，计算器还会给出 pOH、[H⁺] 和 [OH⁻]，让你完整了解溶液的平衡状态。请记住，这里的简单公式假设 25 °C 下的理想行为，此时水的离子积 Kw = 1×10⁻¹⁴。高浓度溶液、极端 pH、温度变化以及活度效应都会使真实测量略有偏差，因此应将结果视为准确的教学和规划估算，而不是经过校准的 pH 计的替代品。

howto.heading: 如何使用 pH 计算器
steps: 选择溶液类型：强酸、弱酸、强碱、弱碱或缓冲液。
对于酸和碱，输入 mol/L 的摩尔浓度；对于弱酸弱碱，还要输入 pKa 或 pKb。
对于缓冲液，输入酸的 pKa，以及酸（HA）和其共轭碱（A−）的摩尔浓度。
点击计算，查看溶液的 pH、pOH、[H⁺] 和 [OH⁻]。
点击重置清除输入，或加载示例查看完整计算过程。

items.q: 什么是 pH，如何计算？
items.a: pH 用氢离子浓度的 10 为底负对数来表示酸性：pH = −log₁₀[H⁺]。pH 低于 7 为酸性，7 为中性，高于 7 为碱性。计算器会先求出对应溶液类型的 [H⁺]，再套用这个公式。

items.q: 如何计算弱酸的 pH？
items.a: 对于弱酸，使用 [H⁺] = √(Ka × C)，其中 Ka = 10^(−pKa)，C 是浓度。然后 pH = −log₁₀[H⁺]。输入浓度和 pKa 后，计算器会自动完成。

items.q: 什么是 Henderson-Hasselbalch 方程？
items.a: 它用于计算缓冲液的 pH：pH = pKa + log₁₀([A⁻]/[HA])，其中 [A⁻] 是共轭碱浓度，[HA] 是酸浓度。当两者相等时，pH 等于 pKa，这也是缓冲液效果最好的位置。

items.q: pH 和 pOH 有什么区别？
items.a: pOH 用于表示氢氧根浓度：pOH = −log₁₀[OH⁻]。在 25 °C 的水中，pH + pOH = 14，所以知道其中一个就能求出另一个。计算器会为每种溶液同时显示两者。

items.q: 为什么计算出的 pH 和仪表读数不同？
items.a: 这些公式假设 25 °C 下的理想行为，并对弱酸和弱碱使用近似。温度、离子强度（活度）、极高或极低浓度以及杂质都会让真实读数略有偏差，因此校正过的 pH 计可能与理想值稍有不同。

items.q: pH 可以是负数或大于 14 吗？
items.a: 可以。0–14 适用于大多数日常溶液，但非常浓的强酸可能出现负 pH，非常浓的强碱则可能超过 14。只要浓度符合，计算器就会回传这些值。

about.heading: 关于英亩转公顷计算器
about.body:
英亩转公顷计算器是一款精确的土地面积计算器，使用国际通行系数 1 英亩 = 0.40468564224 公顷，在英亩与公顷之间进行换算。反向换算使用 1 公顷 = 2.4710538147 英亩。两个数值都来自精确关系：1 国际英亩 = 4,046.8564224 平方米，因此结果与全球土地测量师、土木工程师和政府地籍机构的标准一致。

英亩和公顷表示的是同一种物理量——面积——但它们属于两种截然不同的计量传统。英亩源自中世纪英格兰农业，原本表示一对耕牛一天能犁出的土地面积。如今它仍在美国、英国、印度（并与 bigha、gunta 等本地单位并存）、巴基斯坦、孟加拉国以及部分英联邦国家使用。1 英亩等于 43,560 平方英尺、4,840 平方码，或 1/640 平方英里。相比之下，公顷诞生于法国大革命时期的公制改革，明确定义为 10,000 平方米，也就是 100 m × 100 m 的正方形。它是欧盟所有国家、中国、俄罗斯、巴西、加拿大（房产中也常同时使用英亩）、澳大利亚以及几乎所有非洲和亚洲农业部门的官方土地面积单位。

由于这两种单位分别位于英制和公制体系的边界之间，它们之间的换算需求非常频繁。美国农民为出口合同给大豆地块定价时，即使地块登记单位是英亩，也必须把产量报价转换为每公顷吨数。欧洲房地产基金收购得克萨斯牧场时，需要把挂牌面积换算成公顷，才能与波尔多葡萄园或安达卢西亚橄榄园进行比较。比较美国玉米、巴西大豆、英国小麦和乌克兰小麦生产率的农业经济学家，也必须先把所有数据统一到同一单位，数字才有可比性。这个计算器只需一次乘法，就能让你在两种体系之间切换，而不会因四舍五入出错。

在大多数实际场景中，保留两到四位小数就足够了。0.25 英亩的住宅地块会变成 0.1012 公顷，这对于规划申请已经足够准确。100 英亩的家庭农场会变成 40.4686 公顷，与欧洲常见的中型农场概念相当吻合。5,000 英亩的牧场会变成 2,023.43 公顷，可立即与阿根廷或澳大利亚的大型庄园比较。若需要更高精度——例如在国家地籍登记或 GIS 工作中——该计算器会在内部保留完整的十位有效数字，只在显示时进行四舍五入。

理解结果和计算结果同样重要。1 公顷大约相当于一个国际标准橄榄球场，或约 2.47 英亩。1 英亩约为 4,047 平方米，大小大致相当于不含端区的美式足球场。如果你能在脑海中想象这两个参照形状，就能很快对大多数换算结果做出合理判断。这里使用的精确系数（0.40468564224）来自美国国家标准与技术研究院（NIST）和英国国家物理实验室（NPL）的公开标准，确保结果与测量报告、政府记录和国际贸易文件保持法律一致。

howto.heading: 如何将英亩转换为公顷
howto.step1: 确定起始单位。查看源文件——美国产权契约、MLS 房源和 USDA 报告通常使用英亩；欧盟土地登记、FAO 统计和大多数科学论文通常使用公顷。
howto.step2: 在计算器中选择换算方向：英亩 → 公顷，或公顷 → 英亩。
howto.step3: 输入数值，并尽量保留源数据提供的精度。测量报告建议保留四位或更多小数；一般估算保留两位即可。
howto.step4: 点击“换算”。计算器会分别乘以 0.40468564224（英亩转公顷）或 2.4710538147（公顷转英亩），并显示四舍五入后的结果以及所用公式。
howto.step5: 快速核对结果：1 公顷 ≈ 2.47 英亩，因此同一块土地的公顷数应始终小于英亩数。

faq.heading: 常见问题
faq.q1: 英亩到公顷的精确换算系数是多少？
faq.q1_a: 1 国际英亩正好等于 0.40468564224 公顷，因为 1 英亩定义为 4,046.8564224 平方米，而 1 公顷正好是 10,000 平方米。反向系数 1 公顷 = 2.4710538147 英亩只是它的倒数。
faq.q2: 美国英亩和英国英亩一样吗？
faq.q2_a: 对于日常工作来说，是一样的。两国现在都使用 4,046.8564224 平方米的国际英亩。历史上的美国测量英亩约为 4,046.8726 平方米，差异约为百万分之 16——除高精度美国联邦土地测量外，对几乎所有用途都可忽略。
faq.q3: 公顷和英亩相比有多大？
faq.q3_a: 1 公顷大约是 1 英亩的 2.47 倍。换句话说，1 英亩约为 1 公顷的 40%。公顷是边长 100 米的正方形，而英亩大致相当于不含端区的美式足球场。
faq.q4: 如果我想换算成平方米或平方英尺怎么办？
faq.q4_a: 英亩乘以 4,046.8564224 可得平方米，乘以 43,560 可得平方英尺。公顷乘以 10,000 可得平方米，乘以 107,639.104 可得平方英尺。这个计算器专注于英亩 ↔ 公顷换算，因为这最常见于国际土地交易。
faq.q5: 结果应保留多少位小数？
faq.q5_a: 房地产信息和农场规划保留两到四位小数就足够。地籍申报、GIS 工作或法律文件建议至少保留六位小数，或者直接使用原始值。计算器内部保留十位有效数字，因此你在继续做后续计算时不会损失精度。
faq.q6: 为什么不同来源给出的换算系数会略有不同？
faq.q6_a: 大多数差异都来自四舍五入。有些来源会写成 0.4047 或 0.40469，这用于日常场景没问题，但在大面积地块上会损失精度。英亩转公顷计算器使用 NIST 发布的精确系数 0.40468564224，因此 10,000 英亩的庄园会准确换算为 4,046.8564224 公顷，不会产生累计舍入误差。

loan-calculator.intro: 输入贷款金额、利率和期限，再加上可选的首付或额外月供，就能看到你要支付多少，以及多久可以还清债务。

result.heading: 结果
result.payoff: 如果每月多还一些，这笔贷款可在 {years} 年 {months} 个月后还清。

about.heading: 关于贷款计算器
about.body:
贷款计算器会把几个简单输入——借了多少钱、利率是多少、多久还清——转换成真正重要的数字：你的月供、要付给贷款方的总利息，以及贷款整个期限内的总还款额。无论你是在评估一笔 30 年期房贷、5 年期车贷、用于债务整合的个人贷款，还是用于购置新设备的商业融资，底层数学都是一样的，而贷款月供计算器能立刻算出来。

这个计算器的核心是固定利率、完全摊销贷款的标准摊销公式：M = P · r · (1 + r)^n / ((1 + r)^n − 1)。其中 P 是本金（贷款金额减去任何首付），r 是周期利率（按月还款时为年利率除以 12），n 是总还款次数（年数乘以 12）。每个月，你的还款一部分用于支付剩余本金上的利息，另一部分用于减少本金。贷款初期，大部分还款是利息；后期则主要是本金。这种比例变化就叫摊销。

结果会告诉你三个关键点。月供是你每个月必须承担的金额，也是预算中最直接的支出。总利息就是借钱的成本——对于长期房贷，它甚至可能接近或超过借款本金。总还款额则是本金加利息，是贷款在整个生命周期里的真实总成本。把不同利率和期限放在一起比较，最能看出为什么更低的利率或更短的期限可以节省数万美元。

两个可选输入会让结果更准确。首付会直接减少本金，从而同时降低月供和总利息。额外月供则会在每个月直接冲抵本金，加快余额下降速度；计算器会重新摊销贷款，告诉你能提前多少个月还清，以及能省下多少利息。即使是在长期贷款中，每月只多还一点，也可能缩短好几年。

你可以用贷款计算器来比较不同贷款机构的报价，在 15 年和 30 年期房贷之间做选择，测试把月供四舍五入多付一点值不值得，或者在签字前先看懂报价。请记住，它只估算本金和利息——现实中的月供还可能包含房产税、房屋保险或 PMI、HOA 会费以及贷款手续费，所以请把结果当作规划基准，而不是最终成交金额。

howto.heading: 如何使用贷款计算器
steps: 输入贷款金额——也就是你想借的总额，不含任何首付。
输入年利率（例如 5.5 表示 5.5%）以及贷款期限（年）。
可选地加入首付以降低本金，再加入额外月供以更快还清贷款。
点击“计算”查看你的月供、总利息和总还款额。
调整利率或期限后重新计算，用来比较不同方案，或点击“重置”重新开始。

items.q: 月供是怎么计算的？
items.a: 计算器使用标准摊销公式 M = P · r · (1 + r)^n / ((1 + r)^n − 1)，其中 P 是本金，r 是月利率（年利率 ÷ 12），n 是月供次数（年数 × 12）。这样就能算出在期限结束时刚好还清贷款的固定月供。

items.q: 计算器包含税费和保险吗？
items.a: 不包含。它只估算本金和利息。真实房贷通常还包括房产税、房屋保险或 PMI、HOA 会费以及贷款手续费。你可以把这些另外加上，估算完整的每月住房成本。

items.q: 额外月供有什么帮助？
items.a: 每一笔额外还款都会直接冲抵本金，因此余额下降得比计划更快，累积利息也更少。计算器会根据你的额外月供重新摊销贷款，显示能提前多少个月还清以及能省多少钱。

items.q: 首付字段有什么作用？
items.a: 首付会从贷款金额中扣除，得到你实际融资的本金。首付越高，月供越低，整个贷款期限内支付的总利息也越少。

items.q: 应该选短期还是长期贷款？
items.a: 短期贷款的月供更高，但总利息少得多，因为借款时间更短。长期贷款虽然月供更低，但总利息更高。你可以用计算器比较两者，选择最适合预算的方案。

items.q: 这个计算器适用于所有贷款类型吗？
items.a: 是的，适用于任何固定利率、完全摊销的贷款——包括房贷、车贷、个人贷款以及大多数商业贷款。它不适用于浮动利率贷款、只还利息贷款，或像信用卡那样的循环余额产品，因为这些产品的还款规则不同。

ohms-law-calculator.hint: 请只输入两个值，其余两个留空。

error.invalid: 请准确输入两个值（并确保数值为有效正数），这样计算器才能求出其余两个。

about.heading: 关于欧姆定律计算器
about.body:
欧姆定律是电子学中最重要的关系式。它由德国物理学家乔治·西蒙·欧姆于 1827 年提出，指出通过导体两点之间的电流与两端电压成正比，与电阻成反比。它的常见形式是 V = I × R，其中 V 表示电压（伏特），I 表示电流（安培），R 表示电阻（欧姆，Ω）。这个简洁的公式让你在已知另外两个量时求出任意一个量，因此几乎所有工程师、技术人员和爱好者进行电路计算时都会用到它。

欧姆定律计算器在核心关系之外，还加入了电功率公式 P = V × I，单位为瓦特。将两者结合后，可得到一组等价表达式：P = I² × R 和 P = V² / R。电压、电流、电阻和功率这四个量共同构成一个封闭系统，已知其中任意两个，就能推导出另外两个。只需输入准确的两个值，欧姆定律计算器就会自动选择正确公式，因此你不必记忆该用哪一种变形。

每种情况背后的计算都很直接。若已知电压和电流，电阻就是 V ÷ I，功率就是 V × I。若已知电压和电阻，电流就是 V ÷ R，功率就是 V² ÷ R。若已知电流和电阻，电压就是 I × R，功率就是 I² × R。当功率是已知值之一时，计算器会在需要时使用平方根——例如，若已知电阻和功率，则电流为 √(P ÷ R)，电压为 √(P × R)。所有结果都采用双精度运算，并经过四舍五入以便阅读。

理解这些数值的含义和计算结果同样重要。电压是推动电荷通过电路的“电势压力”；电流是电荷流动的速率；电阻会阻碍这种流动，并把电能转化为热；功率则是能量传递或耗散的速率。电阻器的功率额定值、LED 的串联电阻、电池续航时间以及电机负载都可以用这四个量来分析。例如，选择额定功率足够高的电阻器，就直接取决于这里算出的 P = I² × R。

典型用途包括为 LED 选取限流电阻、检查电阻是否会过热、估算负载从电池或电源汲取多少电流、设计分压电路，以及排查测量值与预期不符的电路。请注意，欧姆定律适用于恒温下的欧姆性（线性）元件；半导体、灯泡和其他非线性器件只会近似符合，因此这里的结果应视为真实元件的理想化估算。

howto.heading: 如何使用欧姆定律计算器
steps: 先找出电路或元件数据表中你已经知道的两个电气量。
将这两个值输入对应的字段：电压 (V)、电流 (I)、电阻 (R) 或功率 (P)。
其余两个字段保持空白——计算器必须恰好输入两个值才能求解电路。
点击“计算”，查看全部四个数值，包括通过欧姆定律和功率公式推导出的两个值。
点击“重置”清空字段，或加载示例以立即查看一个计算实例。

items.q: 什么是欧姆定律？
items.a: 欧姆定律指出电压等于电流乘以电阻：V = I × R。它描述了电路中电压、电流和电阻之间的关系，并且当你知道其中两个时，可以计算第三个。

items.q: 如何用欧姆定律计算功率？
items.a: 电功率公式是 P = V × I。结合欧姆定律后，也可写成 P = I² × R 或 P = V² / R，因此你可以根据电压、电流和电阻中的任意两个来计算功率。

items.q: 需要输入多少个值？
items.a: 恰好两个。只要输入电压、电流、电阻或功率中的任意两个，计算器就能求出另外两个。只输入一个或输入全部四个都不会得到有效结果。

items.q: 欧姆定律计算器使用什么单位？
items.a: 电压使用伏特 (V)，电流使用安培 (A)，电阻使用欧姆 (Ω)，功率使用瓦特 (W)——这些都是标准 SI 单位。输入前请先把毫安换算为安培（除以 1000），把千欧换算为欧姆（乘以 1000）。

items.q: 为什么我的真实元件与计算结果不一致？
items.a: 欧姆定律适用于恒温下的理想欧姆性元件。LED、二极管、白炽灯和其他非线性器件会随电压或温度改变电阻，因此实际测量值可能与这里显示的理想值不同。

items.q: 如何确定电阻的功率额定值？
items.a: 先用 P = I² × R 或 P = V² / R 计算电阻耗散的功率，然后选择额定值明显高于该数值的电阻——通常至少高一倍。对于 1.44 W，选择 2 W 或更高的电阻更安全。

about.heading: 关于标准差计算器
about.body:
标准差是衡量一组数字离散程度最常用的指标。它告诉你每个数值平均离均值有多远。标准差小，说明数据点紧密聚集在平均值附近；标准差大，说明数据分布更分散。由于标准差与原始数据使用相同单位，因此更容易理解，也是统计学、质量控制、金融、科学和社会科学的基础。

计算过程很清晰。先求所有数值的均值（平均数），再用每个数值减去均值并将结果平方——平方可以消除负号，并让偏差更大的数值获得更高权重。把这些平方偏差相加，得到总平方误差。再用数据点个数（或个数减一）去除这个总和，得到方差，最后对方差开平方，就回到了原始单位。这个平方根就是标准差。

这个计算器最重要的选择，是使用样本公式还是总体公式。当你的数据包含你关心群体中的每一个成员时，使用总体公式，也就是除以 n，例如某个部门所有员工的年龄。当你的数据只是更大总体中的一个样本，并且你想估计该总体的离散程度时，使用样本公式，也就是除以 n − 1。除以 n − 1（称为贝塞尔校正）可以使样本标准差成为无偏估计量，这也是大多数统计工作默认使用它的原因。对于同一组数据，样本标准差总是会比总体标准差略大。

除了标准差之外，计算器还会报告方差（离散程度的平方形式）、均值、数量、总和以及最小值和最大值，让你一眼就能看出范围。方差本身也很有用——它具有可加性，并且是方差分析和投资组合风险模型等方法的基础——但由于标准差与数据单位一致，通常更直观，也更适合直接引用。

标准差无处不在：老师用它查看考试成绩是否稳定，制造商用它控制产品重量是否在公差范围内，投资者把它视为收益波动，科学家则用它表示测量的不确定性。在近似正态分布中，大约 68% 的数值落在均值一个标准差范围内，大约 95% 的数值落在两个标准差范围内，因此它在置信区间、z 分数和假设检验中都非常重要。请输入上面的数字，一次算出所有这些统计量。

howto.heading: 如何使用标准差计算器
steps: 在数据框中输入数字，可以用逗号、空格或换行分隔。
如果你的数据只是更大群体的一部分，请选择“样本”；如果包含全部成员，请选择“总体”。
点击“计算”以求出标准差、方差、均值、数量、总和和极差。
查看标准差，判断数值围绕均值的离散程度。
点击“重置”清空数据，或载入一个示例查看完整的示例数据集。

items.q: 什么是标准差？
items.a: 标准差衡量一组数字围绕其均值的离散程度。数值低，表示数据更集中在平均值附近；数值高，表示分布更分散。它与数据使用相同单位，因此很容易理解。

items.q: 样本标准差和总体标准差有什么区别？
items.a: 总体标准差用平方偏差之和除以 n，适用于你的数据覆盖了整个群体的情况。样本标准差用 n − 1（贝塞尔校正）进行除法，适用于用样本去估计更大总体的情况。样本值总是会略大一些。

items.q: 标准差是如何计算的？
items.a: 先求均值，再用每个数值减去均值并平方，把这些平方偏差加总，然后按 n（总体）或 n − 1（样本）求出方差，最后对方差开平方。这个平方根就是标准差。

items.q: 方差和标准差有什么区别？
items.a: 方差是偏离均值的平方偏差的平均值，而标准差是它的平方根。方差使用的是平方单位；标准差与数据单位一致，因此通常更直观，也更适合报告。

items.q: 我的数据应该使用样本还是总体？
items.a: 如果你的数字代表你所关心群体中的每一位成员，就使用总体。如果它们只是更大群体的一部分，并且你想估计整体，就使用样本。面对真实世界的抽样数据时，如果不确定，通常优先使用样本公式。

items.q: 为什么较低的标准差通常被认为更好？
items.a: 这取决于场景。在制造或测试中，较低的标准差表示稳定和可靠。在投资中，较低的标准差意味着更低的波动和风险。较高的标准差只表示更大的变动性，是否有利取决于你的目标。

a1c-calculator-hemoglobin-a1c-to-average.title: A1C计算器：糖化血红蛋白换算平均血糖
a1c-calculator-hemoglobin-a1c-to-average.tagline: 将糖化血红蛋白A1C百分比换算为估算平均血糖(eAG)，同时显示mg/dL和mmol/L，帮助更好管理糖尿病。
a1c-calculator-hemoglobin-a1c-to-average.intro: 输入你最近一次HbA1c结果，即可立即查看过去两到三个月的估算平均血糖，以及它所属的范围。

error.invalid: 请输入有效的糖化血红蛋白A1C百分比（通常在3到20之间）。

about.heading: 关于A1C计算器
about.body:
糖化血红蛋白A1C，也写作HbA1c，是一项血液检测，可反映过去两到三个月的平均血糖。其原理是血液中的葡萄糖会与红细胞内负责携氧的蛋白质血红蛋白结合。平均血糖越高，体内累积的糖化血红蛋白就越多；由于红细胞寿命约为120天，单次A1C读数呈现的是长期图景，而不是某一瞬间的快照。本计算器会把该百分比换算为估算平均葡萄糖(eAG)，便于你与日常血糖仪读数直接比较。

换算采用来自里程碑式ADAG研究并获美国糖尿病协会认可的公式：以mg/dL表示的估算平均葡萄糖等于28.7乘以A1C百分比，再减去46.7。若要用国际单位mmol/L表示同一结果，则将mg/dL数值除以18.0182。因此，A1C为7%大约对应154 mg/dL或8.6 mmol/L。同时报告两种单位，可让结果适用于使用mg/dL的国家（如美国）以及使用mmol/L的国家和地区（如英国、加拿大和欧洲大部分地区）。

理解数值与计算数值同样重要。按照标准诊断阈值，A1C低于5.7%通常视为正常，5.7%至6.4%提示糖尿病前期，6.5%或更高则属于糖尿病范围。对于多数已经患有糖尿病的成年人，常见管理目标是低于7%；但你的个人目标应由医疗服务提供者根据年龄、其他疾病以及低血糖风险等因素设定，可能更高也可能更低。计算器会标出输入值落入哪个范围，帮助你一眼了解临床背景。

了解eAG有助于连接实验室数值和你每天在血糖仪或连续血糖监测仪上看到的葡萄糖数字。如果A1C提示平均血糖约为154 mg/dL，但你的空腹读数一直更低，差异可能来自你未捕捉到的餐后血糖峰值。连续追踪多次A1C检测对应的eAG，是判断饮食、活动或用药改变是否真正推动长期控制向好发展的最清晰方式之一。

还需要记住一些注意事项。eAG公式是统计估算，因此个人真实平均值可能与计算值不同。某些情况——贫血、近期失血或输血、妊娠、肾病以及部分血红蛋白变异——会降低A1C结果的可靠性。A1C计算器仅用于教育和自我追踪，不能替代专业医疗建议；请务必与合格临床医生讨论你的结果和治疗计划。

howto.heading: 如何使用A1C计算器

items.q: A1C检测测量的是什么？
items.a: A1C测量血红蛋白中附着葡萄糖的比例，反映过去两到三个月的平均血糖。由于红细胞寿命约为120天，它提供的是长期视角，而不是某一时刻的读数。

items.q: A1C如何换算为平均血糖？
items.a: 本计算器使用ADA认可的公式：估算平均葡萄糖(mg/dL) = 28.7 × A1C − 46.7。将mg/dL结果除以18.0182即可得到mmol/L数值。例如，A1C为7%约等于154 mg/dL或8.6 mmol/L。

items.q: 正常A1C水平是多少？
items.a: A1C低于5.7%视为正常，5.7%–6.4%提示糖尿病前期，6.5%或更高属于糖尿病范围。许多糖尿病成年人目标是低于7%，但目标因人而异，应与医生共同设定。

items.q: 为什么我的A1C与日常血糖仪读数不一致？
items.a: A1C反映的是平均值，包括夜间和餐后等你可能没有检测的时段。如果血糖仪读数看起来不错但A1C偏高，常见原因是餐后未被发现的血糖峰值。连续血糖监测仪可以揭示这些模式。

items.q: A1C应该多久检测一次？
items.a: 大多数血糖稳定且控制良好的糖尿病患者约每年检测两次；正在调整治疗或未达到目标者可能每三个月检测一次。医疗服务提供者会根据你的情况建议检测频率。

items.q: 这个计算器能替代医疗建议吗？
items.a: 不能。eAG数值是教育用途的估算，贫血、妊娠或血红蛋白变异等情况可能让A1C可靠性下降。请务必与合格医疗专业人员讨论结果和任何治疗决定。

about.heading: 关于百分比计算器
about.body:
百分比本质上就是以一百为基准表示的分数。比如你说“15 percent”，意思就是每一百份中的十五份——写作 15% 或 0.15。由于分母固定，百分比可以用来比较不同总量中的比例，所以它是定价、税费、统计、成绩、金融、科学以及日常购物决策的核心。

这个百分比计算器处理的是现实中绝大多数百分比问题的三种类型。第一种是“X 是 Y 的百分之几？”——用 Y 乘以 X 再除以 100。可用于计算销售税、小费、佣金、折扣金额、加价，或已知总量中的任意份额。第二种是“X 是 Y 的百分之几？”——用 X 除以 Y 再乘以 100。适合你已经知道部分和整体，需要把部分转成百分比时，例如把 50 分中的 45 分整理成清晰的 90%。第三种是“X 是 Y% 的多少？”——用 X 除以 Y 再乘以 100。可用于反推折扣前的原价，或在只知道某个百分比时反算基数。

这些公式是刻意设计成对称的。如果把百分比记作 P，部分记作 A，整体记作 B，那么基本关系始终是 A = (P / 100) × B。三个计算模式只是把这个关系重新排列，以求出缺少的那个变量。内部计算采用标准 IEEE-754 双精度浮点运算，因此对于任何实际输入，结果都能精确到十位有效数字。

也要留意几个常见误区。先打 20% 折扣，再涨 20%，并不会回到原价——因为不同基数上的百分比不会互相抵消。同样，从 4% 变到 5%，既可以说是增加了 1 个百分点，也可以说是相对增加了 25%；两者都正确，但回答的是不同问题。当你叠加百分比时——比如打折后再收销售税、分期复利，或在含税账单上计算小费——一定要按真实发生的顺序逐步对当前总额计算，不要把百分比简单相加。

无论你是在做预算、评分、给商品定价、计算餐厅小费、比较同比增长、计算体脂比例，还是准备数学考试，这个百分比计算器的三种模式几乎能覆盖你会遇到的所有百分比问题。把页面加入书签，让它帮你完成算术，你就能专注于数字本身的含义。

faq.heading: 百分比计算器常见问题
faq.q1: 如何手动计算一个数的百分比？
faq.q1_a: 先把百分比除以 100 转成小数，再乘以这个数。比如 150 的 25%，就是 0.25 × 150 = 37.5。这个计算器内部对任何百分比和任何数字都是这样处理的。
faq.q2: 百分比和百分点有什么区别？
faq.q2_a: 百分点是两个百分比之间的算术差。例如从 4% 到 5% 是增加了 1 个百分点，但相对增长是 25%（1 ÷ 4 = 0.25）。把这两者混淆，是新闻标题中常见的误导来源。
faq.q3: 如何算出折扣前的原价？
faq.q3_a: 使用“X 是 Y% 的多少？”模式。如果售价是 60 美元，且它相当于原价的 80%（也就是 20% 折扣），输入 60 和 80；计算器会返回 75。通用公式是 original = sale ÷ (1 − discount%/100)。
faq.q4: 如何给账单加税或加小费？
faq.q4_a: 使用“X 是 Y 的百分之几？”模式先算出税费或小费金额，再加回原账单。对于 78.50 美元账单的 18% 小费，计算器会返回 14.13 美元，所以总额是 92.63 美元。若想一步得到含小费总额，只要把账单乘以 1.18。
faq.q5: 百分比可以超过 100 吗？
faq.q5_a: 可以。100% 以上只表示超过整体。例如一只股票从 50 美元涨到 150 美元，相当于相对起点增长了 200%（增加 100 美元是 50 美元的 200%）。这个计算器可直接处理大于 100% 的数值，无需特殊设置。
faq.q6: 这些计算对金融和会计足够准确吗？
faq.q6_a: 结果精确到十位有效数字，足以满足零售定价、税费、小费、成绩和日常金融用途。对于需要遵循法定舍入规则的会计工作，务必按你所在地区的舍入规范处理显示结果。

about.heading: About the Expanded Form Calculator
about.body:
Expanded form is a way of writing a number so that every non-zero digit is shown according to its place value. Instead of seeing 2,475 as one block of digits, expanded form rewrites it as 2,000 + 400 + 70 + 5. This makes the structure of the number visible at a glance: the 2 is in the thousands place, the 4 is in the hundreds place, the 7 is in the tens place, and the 5 is in the ones place. Teachers use expanded form in elementary arithmetic because it helps students understand how the base-ten number system works rather than memorising digits mechanically.

The same idea extends naturally to decimals. A number such as 3.14 can be rewritten as 3 + 0.1 + 0.04. In that expression, the 1 is worth one tenth and the 4 is worth four hundredths. Expanded notation therefore connects whole-number place value to decimal place value in one consistent framework. That matters when students move from counting and addition into subtraction with regrouping, multiplication, decimals, fractions, and eventually algebra. If you understand place value, many later procedures feel logical instead of arbitrary.

This expanded form calculator automates that breakdown for integers, decimals, and negative values. It removes commas and spaces, reads the sign, isolates the integer and decimal parts, and then builds each place-value component one by one. Zeros are skipped because they do not contribute a non-zero value to the sum. For example, 5,060 becomes 5,000 + 60 rather than 5,000 + 0 + 60 + 0. For negative numbers, the calculator preserves the same decomposition but wraps the result in parentheses, so -105.2 becomes -(100 + 5 + 0.2). That layout keeps the sign clear and avoids ambiguity.

Expanded form is useful far beyond a classroom worksheet. It can help parents explain homework, allow tutors to demonstrate regrouping strategies, and give adult learners a quick refresher on place value before revisiting basic math. It also supports estimation: once you can see the individual place values, rounding and mental arithmetic become easier. Engineers, accountants, and programmers may not write numbers in expanded notation every day, but the underlying place-value logic still drives numeric representation in spreadsheets, code, and scientific notation.

Because the calculator shows the final sum instantly, it is a helpful checking tool as well as a teaching aid. You can test your own answer, compare similar numbers, or explore how moving a digit left or right changes its value by a factor of ten. Whether you are learning the concept for the first time or reviewing it after years away from school, the expanded form calculator gives you a fast, readable way to see exactly how a number is built.

items.q: What is expanded form in math?
items.a: Expanded form rewrites a number as the sum of the value of each non-zero digit. It shows how the base-ten system works by making each place value visible instead of leaving the number compressed into a single string of digits.

items.q: Why are zeros usually left out of expanded form?
items.a: A zero in any place contributes no additional value to the total, so it does not need its own term. For example, 5,060 is usually written as 5,000 + 60, not 5,000 + 0 + 60 + 0.

items.q: Can decimals be written in expanded form?
items.a: Yes. Decimal digits are written as tenths, hundredths, thousandths, and so on, which is why 3.14 becomes 3 + 0.1 + 0.04. This is one of the best ways to understand what each decimal place actually means.

items.q: How does expanded form work for negative numbers?
items.a: The place-value decomposition is found from the absolute value first, then the minus sign is applied to the entire sum. Writing -105.2 as -(100 + 5 + 0.2) makes it clear that every term belongs to the same negative quantity.

items.q: Is expanded form the same as scientific notation?
items.a: No. Expanded form writes every non-zero place-value contribution explicitly, while scientific notation rewrites a number as a coefficient times a power of ten. Both rely on place value, but they are used for different learning goals and problem types.

expanding-logarithms-calculator.title: Expanding Logarithms Calculator
expanding-logarithms-calculator.tagline: Apply the product, quotient, and power rules for logarithms with natural log, common log, or a custom base and see both the symbolic expansion and numeric value.
expanding-logarithms-calculator.intro: Choose a logarithm type and rule, enter the values, and the calculator rewrites the expression step by step while also evaluating it numerically when the inputs are valid.

error.invalid: Please enter valid logarithm inputs. Arguments must be positive, denominators must be positive, and a custom base must be positive and not equal to 1.

about.heading: About the Expanding Logarithms Calculator
about.body:
The expanding logarithms calculator helps you apply the three core identities that make logarithms manageable in algebra, precalculus, and calculus: the product rule, the quotient rule, and the power rule. These rules let you transform a log of a multiplication, division, or exponent into a sum, difference, or coefficient. That matters because expanded logarithms are often easier to simplify, differentiate, integrate, compare, or solve inside equations. Instead of treating a logarithm as a black box, expansion exposes the structure of the expression.

The product rule says log(mn) = log(m) + log(n), provided the argument values are positive. The quotient rule says log(m/n) = log(m) - log(n). The power rule says log(m^n) = n log(m). All three rules come from the same exponent laws. Because logarithms are inverse functions of exponentials, multiplication inside a logarithm turns into addition outside, division turns into subtraction, and an exponent on the argument becomes a coefficient in front of the logarithm. These identities are true for natural logarithms, common logarithms, and any other valid base greater than zero and not equal to one.

This calculator uses a practical interface instead of a full symbolic parser. You choose the logarithm type and one of the three standard rules, then enter the relevant numeric values. For example, you can expand log(2·8) into log(2) + log(8), rewrite ln(9/3) as ln(9) - ln(3), or transform log₂(8^3) into 3·log₂(8). Once the symbolic step is displayed, the calculator also evaluates the expression numerically so that you can check the identity with actual numbers. That combination is especially useful when studying because it connects the algebraic rule to the resulting value.

It is important to remember the domain restrictions. Logarithm arguments must stay positive. You cannot take the real logarithm of zero or a negative number, so the calculator rejects invalid arguments before displaying a result. For custom bases, the base must also be positive and cannot equal 1, because log base 1 is undefined. These conditions often appear on exams, and forgetting them is one of the most common mistakes when expanding or condensing logarithmic expressions.

Use this calculator to verify homework, build intuition, or quickly demonstrate log properties during tutoring. It does not replace symbolic proof, but it gives you a reliable step-by-step checkpoint. If you are preparing for SAT Math, ACT, AP Precalculus, college algebra, or calculus, mastering these rules is essential. The expanding logarithms calculator makes that practice faster, clearer, and easier to review.

examples.heading: Examples
examples.intro: These sample cases show the three main logarithm rules in action with different log types.

items.note: Product rule: multiplication inside the logarithm becomes addition outside.
Quotient rule: division inside the logarithm becomes subtraction.
Power rule: the exponent moves to the front as a coefficient.
The power rule works for common logarithms as well as custom bases and natural logs.

howto.heading: How to use
steps: Choose the logarithm type: natural log, common log, or custom base log. If you pick custom base, enter a base greater than 0 and not equal to 1.
Select the logarithm rule you want to apply: product, quotient, or power.
Enter the values required by that rule. Product and quotient use two positive arguments, while the power rule uses a positive argument and any real exponent.
Click Calculate Expansion to display the symbolic rewrite and the numeric value of the expression.
Use Reset to return to the default common-log product form and start a new example.

items.q: Why do logarithm expansions require positive arguments?
items.a: In the real-number system, logarithms are only defined for positive arguments. That is why expressions such as log(0) or log(-3) are invalid in this calculator and in standard algebra courses.

items.q: Does the product rule work for every logarithm base?
items.a: Yes. The product, quotient, and power rules hold for natural logs, common logs, and any custom base b where b > 0 and b ≠ 1. The base changes the numeric value, but not the structure of the rule itself.

items.q: What is the difference between expanding and condensing logarithms?
items.a: Expanding logarithms uses the rules to split one logarithm into multiple terms. Condensing logarithms does the reverse by combining sums, differences, and coefficients into a single logarithm.

items.q: Why is log base 1 undefined?
items.a: A logarithm asks what exponent makes the base produce a target value. Because 1 raised to any power is still 1, base 1 cannot produce a unique answer for other positive numbers, so log base 1 is undefined.

items.q: Can the exponent in the power rule be negative or fractional?
items.a: Yes, as long as the logarithm argument itself remains positive. The calculator allows any real exponent in the power rule because n·log(m) is valid whenever m > 0.

exponent-calculator.title: Exponent Calculator
exponent-calculator.tagline: Raise any real base to any real exponent, see the expression written clearly, and handle special cases such as 0^0 or overflow in one step.
exponent-calculator.intro: Enter a base and an exponent to evaluate powers such as 2^10, 9^0.5, or 0^0, with instant feedback for invalid real-number results.

error.invalid: Please enter valid real-number inputs. Some negative bases with fractional exponents produce complex values, which this calculator does not display.

about.heading: About the Exponent Calculator
about.body:
An exponent tells you how many times a base is used in repeated multiplication or, more generally, how a quantity scales. In the expression 2^10, the base is 2 and the exponent is 10, so the result is 2 multiplied by itself ten times. Exponents appear everywhere in mathematics because they compress long repeated products into a short, readable notation. They are central to algebra, geometry, finance, statistics, computing, and the natural sciences.

Whole-number exponents are the easiest place to start. A positive integer exponent such as 3^4 means 3 × 3 × 3 × 3 = 81. An exponent of 1 leaves the base unchanged, while an exponent of 0 gives 1 for every non-zero base. Negative exponents reverse the operation and create reciprocals, so 2^-3 = 1 / 2^3 = 1/8. Fractional exponents introduce roots: 9^0.5 is the square root of 9, and 27^(1/3) is the cube root of 27. Once you understand these patterns, exponent rules become a powerful shortcut for simplifying many expressions.

This exponent calculator evaluates b^n directly using standard real-number arithmetic. It follows the common classroom convention that 0^0 = 1, which is useful in many discrete-math and programming contexts even though the expression requires care in higher analysis. The calculator also handles very large results. If the computed value overflows JavaScript's finite number range, the display shows infinity. That makes the tool practical for quick checks even when the magnitude of the answer becomes enormous.

There is one important real-number limitation: a negative base raised to a fractional exponent may not have a real result in standard floating-point arithmetic. For example, (-8)^(1/3) has a real cube-root interpretation in pure mathematics, but `Math.pow` can return NaN because the exponent is represented as a floating-point fraction rather than an exact rational number. For that reason, the calculator reports these cases as invalid instead of pretending there is a simple real answer. This protects you from reading a misleading output when the expression actually belongs in the complex-number system or needs special handling.

Use this calculator when checking homework, verifying spreadsheet formulas, exploring growth and decay, or reviewing exponent rules before an exam. It gives a direct answer, but it also reinforces the notation by showing the original expression alongside the result. Whether you are working on algebra practice, compound-interest problems, population growth, scientific notation, or basic programming math, the exponent calculator provides a fast and dependable way to evaluate powers.

howto.heading: How to use

items.q: What does an exponent mean?
items.a: An exponent tells you how many times to use the base in multiplication or how strongly to scale a number. It is a compact notation for repeated multiplication and its extensions to reciprocals and roots.

items.q: Why does any non-zero number to the power 0 equal 1?
items.a: This follows from the exponent rule a^m / a^m = a^(m-m) = a^0. Because any non-zero number divided by itself equals 1, the expression a^0 must also equal 1.

items.q: Why can negative bases with fractional exponents fail?
items.a: Some of those expressions belong to the complex-number system or need exact rational-exponent handling to produce a real root. Because floating-point exponent input is approximate, the calculator treats NaN results as invalid rather than guessing.

items.q: Why does this calculator define 0^0 as 1?
items.a: Many areas of discrete math, combinatorics, and programming use 0^0 = 1 because it makes formulas and series expansions behave consistently in practical contexts. In advanced analysis the expression is more delicate, which is why textbooks sometimes discuss it separately.

items.q: What happens when the result is too large to fit in a normal number?
items.a: The calculator shows infinity when the underlying floating-point result overflows. That tells you the power is larger in magnitude than JavaScript's finite number range can represent directly.

exponential-form-calculator.title: Exponential Form Calculator
exponential-form-calculator.tagline: Convert ordinary numbers into scientific notation or turn exponential form back into standard notation with clear, readable results.
exponential-form-calculator.intro: Use the two-way converter to switch between standard numbers such as 1,234,000 and exponential notation such as 1.234 × 10^6.

error.invalid: Please enter a valid number for standard notation, or a valid coefficient with an integer exponent for exponential notation.

about.heading: About the Exponential Form Calculator
about.body:
Exponential form, more commonly called scientific notation, is a compact way to write very large or very small numbers. Instead of writing every zero explicitly, you rewrite the number as a coefficient multiplied by a power of ten. For example, 1,234,000 becomes 1.234 × 10^6, and 0.00047 becomes 4.7 × 10^-4. This format makes it much easier to compare magnitudes, preserve significant digits, and avoid reading mistakes when numbers stretch across many places.

The key idea is that the coefficient carries the meaningful digits while the exponent tells you how many places the decimal point has been shifted. A positive exponent means the original number is large because the decimal point moved to the right. A negative exponent means the original number is small because the decimal point moved to the left. Scientific notation is therefore not a different number system; it is simply a clearer way to express the same value using powers of ten.

Scientists, engineers, programmers, statisticians, and finance professionals all rely on exponential notation. Astronomers use it for distances measured in millions or billions of kilometres. Chemists use it for concentrations such as 6.02 × 10^23 particles per mole or 3.2 × 10^-7 mol/L. Computer scientists use it when discussing floating-point precision, storage limits, and performance benchmarks. Even in school math, scientific notation is a standard topic because it trains students to understand place value, powers, and significant figures together.

This calculator works in both directions. In standard-to-exponential mode, it takes a regular number and rewrites it as a coefficient times 10 raised to an integer power. In exponential-to-standard mode, it multiplies the coefficient by the requested power of ten to reconstruct the ordinary decimal number. That means you can use the same tool to simplify a value for presentation or to translate scientific notation back into everyday form for reading, reporting, or checking homework.

The converter is especially helpful when you want a clean answer without manually counting zeros or decimal places. It also reduces mistakes when moving between forms during algebra, physics, chemistry, or spreadsheet work. Whether you are writing a lab report, reviewing a math lesson, interpreting a calculator display, or double-checking numbers copied from a paper, the exponential form calculator gives you a quick and reliable way to move between standard notation and scientific notation.

examples.heading: Examples
examples.intro: These examples show both conversion directions for large and small values.

A large number uses a positive exponent because the decimal point moves right.
Moving from exponential notation back to standard form means shifting the decimal right six places.
A negative exponent moves the decimal left when converting back to standard notation.

howto.heading: How to use
steps: Choose the conversion mode: Standard → Exponential or Exponential → Standard.
In standard mode, enter one ordinary number. In exponential mode, enter a coefficient and an integer exponent.
Click Convert Notation to generate the converted form instantly.
Check the result line to confirm the original number and the converted number match exactly in value.
Use Reset to clear the form and return to the default standard-to-exponential mode.

items.q: What is scientific notation?
items.a: Scientific notation writes a number as a coefficient multiplied by a power of ten. It is a compact way to display very large or very small values without writing long strings of zeros.

items.q: Why must the exponent in scientific notation be an integer?
items.a: In standard scientific notation, the exponent counts how many places the decimal point moves, so it must be a whole number. Non-integer powers of ten belong to exponential expressions, but not to the usual decimal-shift form taught in school science and math.

items.q: What does a negative exponent mean?
items.a: A negative exponent means the value is less than 1, so the decimal point moves to the left when converting back to standard notation. For example, 10^-4 means divide by 10,000.

items.q: Is exponential form the same as engineering notation?
items.a: No. Engineering notation is a variation of scientific notation in which the exponent is always a multiple of three. Scientific notation allows any integer exponent, as long as the coefficient carries the significant digits.

items.q: Why is scientific notation useful?
items.a: It makes large and small numbers easier to read, compare, store, and calculate with. It also helps preserve significant figures, which is especially important in laboratory measurements and technical reports.

exponential-function-calculator.title: Exponential Function Calculator
exponential-function-calculator.tagline: Evaluate exponential functions of the form f(x) = a·b^x + c for any real input x and see the substituted expression and final value instantly.
exponential-function-calculator.intro: Enter the coefficient, base, input value, and vertical shift to compute growth or decay functions in the standard form a·b^x + c.

error.invalid: Please enter valid real numbers for a, x, and c, plus a base b that is greater than 0 and not equal to 1.

about.heading: About the Exponential Function Calculator
about.body:
An exponential function models a quantity that changes by a constant multiplicative factor rather than a constant additive amount. In the form f(x) = a·b^x + c, the parameter a scales the overall size of the function, b controls the growth or decay rate, x is the input, and c shifts the graph vertically. This family of functions appears everywhere in mathematics and applied science because many real processes grow or shrink proportionally to their current size.

The base b is the most important parameter for interpreting behaviour. When b > 1, the function represents exponential growth: each step in x multiplies the previous value by b. When 0 < b < 1, the function represents exponential decay: each step in x shrinks the value by a constant factor. That is why the same formula can describe money growing with compound interest, bacterial populations doubling over time, radioactive substances decaying, cooling curves, and the fading intensity of sound or light.

The coefficient a sets the initial scaling. If x = 0, then b^0 = 1, so the function becomes f(0) = a + c. This gives a quick way to understand the starting level of the model. The vertical shift c then moves the whole graph up or down without changing the underlying exponential factor. In applications, c often represents a baseline or asymptote: an environmental background level, a minimum floor value, or a long-run limiting value the system approaches but never fully crosses.

This calculator evaluates the function numerically for any real x as long as the base satisfies the standard exponential conditions b > 0 and b ≠ 1. Those restrictions are important. A non-positive base breaks the standard real exponential model, and b = 1 collapses the expression into a constant function instead of genuine exponential behaviour. By enforcing the usual rules, the calculator stays aligned with how exponential functions are defined in algebra, precalculus, calculus, and applied modeling.

Use the exponential function calculator to test homework answers, inspect parameter changes, or build intuition about growth and decay. You can compare different values of a, b, x, and c to see how each part affects the output. Whether you are studying graph transformations, checking a finance formula, modeling a population, or reviewing a science problem, this tool gives you a fast and readable way to evaluate f(x) = a·b^x + c.

examples.heading: Examples
examples.intro: These examples show how changing the parameters affects the output of an exponential function.

items.note: A basic doubling function with no vertical shift.
A negative x value produces a reciprocal power because 2^-2 = 1/4.
Decay example: each step halves the powered term before the vertical shift is added.

howto.heading: How to use
steps: Enter the coefficient a, which scales the exponential term. The default starting value is 1.
Enter a base b greater than 0 and not equal to 1, then enter the input value x you want to evaluate.
Optionally adjust the vertical shift c. Leave it at 0 if you want no upward or downward translation.
Click Evaluate Function to substitute the values into f(x) = a·b^x + c and display the result.
Use Reset to return to the default a = 1 and c = 0 values and clear the other fields.

items.q: What is the difference between exponential growth and exponential decay?
items.a: Exponential growth happens when the base b is greater than 1, so the function multiplies upward as x increases. Exponential decay happens when the base lies between 0 and 1, so the function shrinks by a constant factor instead.

items.q: Why can the base not be 1?
items.a: If b = 1, then 1^x is always 1, so the exponential part never changes. That turns the formula into the constant function a + c instead of a true exponential relationship.

items.q: What does the vertical shift c do?
items.a: The value c moves the entire graph up or down without changing the growth or decay factor. In applications it often represents a baseline level or horizontal asymptote offset.

items.q: Why does a negative x sometimes make the value smaller?
items.a: A negative exponent creates a reciprocal power, so b^-x becomes 1 / b^x when b is positive. That usually shrinks the exponential term if the base is greater than 1.

items.q: Where are exponential functions used in real life?
items.a: They appear in compound interest, inflation adjustments, population growth, bacterial doubling, radioactive decay, cooling, signal attenuation, and many other time-based processes. Any system that changes by a constant percentage or factor over equal intervals is a natural candidate for an exponential model.

variance-calculator.title: Variance Calculator - Sample & Population Variance
variance-calculator.tagline: Compute variance, standard deviation, mean, median, mode, and IQR for any data set — choose sample or population formula.
variance-calculator.intro: Enter numbers separated by commas or spaces, choose sample or population, and get a complete statistical summary instantly.
variance-calculator.formula: Sample variance: s² = Σ(xᵢ − x̄)² / (n − 1)  •  Population variance: σ² = Σ(xᵢ − μ)² / n

error.invalid: Enter at least two numbers for sample variance, or one number for population, separated by commas or spaces.

about.heading: About the variance calculator
about.body:
Variance is the average of the squared differences between each data point and the mean of a data set. It quantifies how spread out the values in a distribution are. A variance of zero means every value is identical; a large variance means the data points are widely scattered around the mean. Variance is expressed in squared units, which is why its square root — the standard deviation — is often more intuitive to report, but variance itself is essential in statistical theory because it is additive and underlies many advanced techniques.

The variance calculator distinguishes between two fundamentally different use cases. Population variance (σ²) divides the sum of squared deviations by n, the total count of values. Use it when your data set is the complete population you want to describe — for example, the heights of every student in a single class. Sample variance (s²) divides by n − 1 instead, applying Bessel's correction, which compensates for the fact that a sample's mean is itself an estimate and therefore slightly underestimates the spread of the underlying population. For any finite sample, the corrected value is always slightly larger than the uncorrected one. Whenever your numbers are a sample drawn from a larger group, sample variance is the standard choice.

Beyond variance, this calculator computes a full descriptive-statistics summary. The mean is the arithmetic average: sum divided by count. The median is the middle value when data is sorted, or the average of the two middle values for even-length sets; it is resistant to outliers and often more informative than the mean for skewed distributions. The mode is the value (or values) that appear most frequently; if every number appears once, the data is described as having no mode. The range is the difference between the maximum and minimum values. The interquartile range (IQR) is the spread of the middle 50 percent of data, from the 25th to the 75th percentile, and is especially useful for identifying outliers via the fence method.

Variance and its companions — standard deviation, IQR, and range — are used everywhere data is analyzed. Quality engineers use variance to monitor production consistency and flag batches that deviate from specification. Investment analysts use variance as a measure of portfolio volatility: the higher the variance of returns, the riskier the asset. Educators use it to see whether test scores are clustered tightly (low variance, consistent class) or scattered (high variance, mixed understanding). Epidemiologists use population variance to characterize the distribution of disease incidence across regions, and social scientists use it to compare inequality across demographic groups.

This tool handles any list of numbers — integers, decimals, positive, negative — and computes all statistics in a single step. For very large or very small numbers the results are displayed with up to six significant decimal places to balance readability and accuracy.

Ages of all 8 employees in a department. Mean = 35.125, σ ≈ 8.39. Higher variance because of two outliers at 45 and 51.
Eight days of stock closing prices. Mean ≈ 252.94, s ≈ 2.26. Low variance — prices are tightly clustered.

howto.heading: How to use the variance calculator
steps: Type or paste your numbers into the data field, separated by commas, spaces, or new lines.
Choose Sample if your data is a subset of a larger population, or Population if it includes every member.
Click Calculate to compute variance, standard deviation, mean, median, mode, IQR, and range.
Read the Variance row for the squared spread and the Standard Deviation row for the same spread in the original units.
Click Reset to clear all inputs and start a new calculation, or load an example to see a worked data set.

items.q: What is variance and what does it measure?
items.a: Variance measures how spread out a set of numbers is around their mean. It is calculated as the average of the squared differences between each value and the mean. A higher variance means greater dispersion; a variance of zero means all values are identical.

items.q: What is the difference between sample and population variance?
items.a: Population variance divides by n and is used when your data includes every member of the group. Sample variance divides by n − 1 (Bessel's correction) and is used when your data is a subset drawn from a larger population. The correction prevents underestimating the true population spread.

items.q: How is variance related to standard deviation?
items.a: Standard deviation is the square root of variance. Variance is in squared units (e.g., squared dollars or squared kilograms), which makes it hard to interpret directly. Taking the square root returns the measure to the original units, making standard deviation more intuitive for most comparisons.

items.q: When should I report variance instead of standard deviation?
items.a: Variance is preferred in theoretical work and in techniques like ANOVA, regression, or portfolio theory where additivity matters — the variance of the sum of independent variables equals the sum of their variances. Standard deviation is preferred for communicating spread to a general audience because it shares the data's units.

items.q: What does a high or low IQR indicate?
items.a: The IQR is the range of the middle 50 percent of data. A small IQR means the central values are tightly packed; a large IQR means they are spread out. It is more robust than variance and standard deviation because it ignores extreme outliers that would inflate those measures.

items.q: Can variance be negative?
items.a: No. Variance is the sum of squared terms divided by a positive number, so it is always zero or positive. A variance of zero means all values in the data set are identical. If you see a negative result somewhere, it indicates a calculation error.

venn-diagram-calculator.title: Venn Diagram Calculator - Union, Intersection & Difference

venn-diagram-calculator.formula: 2-set union: |A ∪ B| = |A| + |B| − |A ∩ B|  •  3-set union: |A ∪ B ∪ C| = |A| + |B| + |C| − |A ∩ B| − |A ∩ C| − |B ∩ C| + |A ∩ B ∩ C|

error.invalid: Please enter valid non-negative numbers. Intersections cannot exceed the size of the individual sets.

result.union: Union (A ∪ B or A ∪ B ∪ C)

about.heading: About the Venn diagram calculator
about.body:
A Venn diagram is a visual representation of the relationships between two or more sets. Circles (or ellipses) are drawn so that their overlapping regions correspond to elements that belong to multiple sets simultaneously. Venn diagrams were introduced by the English logician John Venn in 1880 and have since become one of the most widely used tools in mathematics, logic, statistics, computer science, linguistics, and everyday reasoning.

For a 2-set Venn diagram, three regions matter: elements that belong only to A, elements that belong only to B, and elements in the intersection A ∩ B that belong to both. The union A ∪ B is the total count of distinct elements in either set, calculated as |A| + |B| − |A ∩ B|. Subtracting the intersection avoids double-counting the elements that appear in both circles. This formula underlies the inclusion-exclusion principle, which generalises to any number of sets.

For a 3-set Venn diagram, seven distinct regions appear: elements exclusive to A, elements exclusive to B, elements exclusive to C, elements in A ∩ B but not C, elements in A ∩ C but not B, elements in B ∩ C but not A, and elements in the central triple intersection A ∩ B ∩ C. The 3-set union formula is |A| + |B| + |C| − |A ∩ B| − |A ∩ C| − |B ∩ C| + |A ∩ B ∩ C|. The triple intersection is added back in because it was subtracted three times (once for each pairwise intersection) after being added three times (once for each set), so it needs to be restored exactly once.

Practical applications of Venn diagrams are everywhere. Survey analysts use them to decompose audiences: how many respondents use platform A only, platform B only, or both? Database engineers use set operations — UNION, INTERSECT, EXCEPT — that map directly to Venn regions. Medical researchers use them to analyse how many patients exhibit symptom A, symptom B, or both. Educators use them to compare and contrast concepts. Market researchers use them to understand brand overlap. In probability theory, the Venn diagram makes the addition rule — P(A ∪ B) = P(A) + P(B) − P(A ∩ B) — immediately visual and intuitive.

This calculator validates the inputs before computing: it checks that no intersection exceeds the size of its component sets, that the triple intersection does not exceed any pairwise intersection, and that all values are non-negative. If the inputs are consistent, every region of the diagram is computed and displayed in a clear table.

A only = 75, B only = 25, both = 75. Of 175 readers, 75 read both genres — a large overlap.
Central region = 5 people use all three platforms. A∩B only = 25, A∩C only = 15, B∩C only = 10.

howto.heading: How to use the Venn diagram calculator

items.q: What is a Venn diagram?
items.a: A Venn diagram uses overlapping circles to show the logical relationships between sets. The overlap between two circles represents elements shared by both sets (intersection), while the non-overlapping parts represent elements belonging to only one set (exclusive regions).

items.q: What is the formula for the union of two sets?
items.a: The union |A ∪ B| = |A| + |B| − |A ∩ B|. You must subtract the intersection because those elements are counted once in |A| and once in |B|; subtracting |A ∩ B| removes the double count so each element is counted exactly once.

items.q: How does the 3-set union formula work?
items.a: For three sets: |A ∪ B ∪ C| = |A| + |B| + |C| − |A ∩ B| − |A ∩ C| − |B ∩ C| + |A ∩ B ∩ C|. Each element is added three times (once per set), pairwise intersections are each subtracted once, but this over-subtracts the triple intersection by one, so it must be added back.

items.q: What does 'exclusive to A' mean?
items.a: Elements exclusive to A belong to set A but not to any other set. In a 2-set diagram, A only = |A| − |A ∩ B|. In a 3-set diagram, A only = |A| − |A ∩ B| − |A ∩ C| + |A ∩ B ∩ C|, adding back the triple intersection that was removed twice.

items.q: Why does the calculator reject some input combinations?
items.a: The intersection of two sets cannot be larger than either set alone, since the intersection is a subset of both. Similarly, the triple intersection cannot exceed any pairwise intersection. The calculator enforces these constraints to prevent mathematically impossible configurations.

wald-test-calculator.title: Wald Test Calculator - Statistical Significance
wald-test-calculator.tagline: Perform a Wald test to determine whether a parameter estimate is statistically significant — enter β̂, β₀, SE, and α for instant results.
wald-test-calculator.intro: Enter the parameter estimate, the hypothesized value, the standard error, and the significance level. The calculator returns the Wald statistic, z-score, p-value, and decision.
wald-test-calculator.formula: Wald statistic W = ((β̂ − β₀) / SE)²  •  z-score = (β̂ − β₀) / SE  •  p-value = 2 · (1 − Φ(|z|))

error.invalid: Please enter valid numbers. Standard error must be positive and significance level must be between 0 and 1.

about.heading: About the Wald test calculator
about.body:
The Wald test is a parametric statistical test named after the statistician Abraham Wald. It is one of the three classical tests of hypothesis in maximum likelihood estimation, alongside the likelihood-ratio test and the score (Lagrange multiplier) test. The Wald test is by far the most commonly encountered because it requires only the estimate itself and its standard error — both of which are routinely reported by statistical software — without requiring the full likelihood function.

The idea behind the Wald test is straightforward. If a parameter estimate β̂ is far from the hypothesized value β₀ relative to its precision (measured by the standard error SE), then the null hypothesis H₀: β = β₀ is unlikely to be true. The Wald statistic is W = ((β̂ − β₀) / SE)², which is the squared z-score. Under the null hypothesis and with a large enough sample, W follows a chi-squared distribution with one degree of freedom. Equivalently, the signed z-score z = (β̂ − β₀) / SE follows a standard normal distribution, so the two-sided p-value is 2 · (1 − Φ(|z|)), where Φ is the standard normal CDF.

The p-value answers the question: if the null hypothesis were true, what is the probability of observing a test statistic at least as extreme as the one actually computed? A small p-value (typically below the significance level α, often set to 0.05 or 0.01) provides evidence against H₀. When p < α, the result is called statistically significant and we reject the null hypothesis. When p ≥ α, we fail to reject it — we do not have enough evidence to conclude the parameter differs from β₀, though this does not mean H₀ is true.

The Wald test is ubiquitous in applied statistics. In linear and logistic regression, the t-statistics reported for each coefficient are essentially Wald z-scores, and the associated p-values test whether each coefficient is significantly different from zero. In econometrics, the Wald test is used to test joint hypotheses about multiple coefficients simultaneously (using matrix algebra extensions). In survival analysis, it tests whether a covariate significantly predicts the hazard rate. In genetics, genome-wide association studies use Wald-type statistics to test millions of single-nucleotide polymorphisms.

A known limitation of the Wald test is that it can produce different results depending on how a parameter is parameterised, because the test relies on a local quadratic approximation to the likelihood. For small samples, the likelihood-ratio test is often preferred because it is more accurate. The Wald test is most reliable when the sample size is large, the estimate is approximately normally distributed (i.e., asymptotic conditions hold), and the standard error is well-estimated.

examples.heading: Wald test examples
examples.intro: Three realistic scenarios from economics, medicine, and general statistics showing the Wald test in action.

items.note: z = 2.27, W = 5.17, p ≈ 0.023. The estimate is more than 2 standard errors from zero, so we reject the null at α = 0.05.
Education coefficient: z = 4.0, p < 0.001. An additional year of education has a highly significant non-zero effect on wages.
Drug efficacy at strict α=0.01: z = −2.5, p ≈ 0.012. The effect is significant at α=0.05 but not at the stricter 1% threshold.

howto.heading: How to use the Wald test calculator
steps: Enter the parameter estimate β̂ from your regression output or statistical model.
Enter the hypothesized value β₀ — typically 0 when testing whether a coefficient is non-zero.
Enter the standard error SE of the estimate, reported in the same statistical output.
Set the significance level α — use 0.05 for the conventional 5% threshold or 0.01 for a stricter 1% threshold.
Click Calculate to get the Wald statistic, z-score, two-sided p-value, and the reject/fail-to-reject decision.

items.q: What does the Wald test measure?
items.a: The Wald test measures how far a parameter estimate is from a hypothesized value, expressed in units of standard error. It tests whether that distance is large enough to conclude, at a given significance level, that the true parameter differs from the hypothesized value.

items.q: What is the difference between the Wald test and the t-test?
items.a: In large samples they are essentially equivalent — both compare an estimate to a null value in units of standard error. The main difference is that the t-test uses a t-distribution (accounting for uncertainty in the variance estimate), while the Wald test uses the normal distribution and is therefore an asymptotic test best suited to large samples.

items.q: Why is the null hypothesis usually β₀ = 0?
items.a: Testing against zero asks whether a predictor has any effect at all. In regression, a coefficient of zero means the variable is irrelevant. Setting β₀ = 0 is the most common use, but you can test against any value — for example, to check whether a parameter equals a theoretically predicted value such as 1 or −0.5.

items.q: What does failing to reject H₀ mean?
items.a: Failing to reject H₀ means the data do not provide sufficient evidence to conclude that the parameter differs from the hypothesized value. It does not prove H₀ is true. The result may reflect a genuinely zero effect, or it may reflect insufficient statistical power due to a small sample or a large standard error.

items.q: When should I use the likelihood-ratio test instead?
items.a: The likelihood-ratio test is preferred when sample sizes are small, when the parameter is near the boundary of its allowable range, or when the Wald test results depend heavily on the parameterisation chosen. For large samples and smoothly distributed estimates, the Wald test and likelihood-ratio test give nearly identical p-values.

items.q: What significance level should I use?
items.a: The conventional threshold is α = 0.05 (5%), meaning you accept a 5% chance of falsely rejecting a true null hypothesis. For stricter requirements — medical device approval, genomics, or physics — α = 0.01 or even 0.001 is used. For exploratory research, α = 0.10 is sometimes accepted. The choice should be made before seeing the data.

weibull-distribution-calculator.title: Weibull Distribution Calculator - PDF, CDF & Reliability
weibull-distribution-calculator.tagline: Compute the Weibull PDF, CDF, reliability function, hazard rate, mean, median, mode, and variance from any shape and scale parameters.

weibull-distribution-calculator.formula: PDF: f(x) = (k/λ)(x/λ)^(k−1) · exp(−(x/λ)^k)  •  CDF: F(x) = 1 − exp(−(x/λ)^k)  •  Mean = λ · Γ(1 + 1/k)

error.invalid: Please enter valid numbers. Shape k and scale λ must be positive; value x must be non-negative.

about.heading: About the Weibull distribution calculator
about.body:
The Weibull distribution is a continuous probability distribution named after the Swedish engineer and mathematician Waloddi Weibull, who used it in 1951 to model material strength and fatigue. It is now one of the most important distributions in reliability engineering, survival analysis, wind-speed modeling, and extreme-value theory because its shape parameter k allows it to model increasing, constant, or decreasing failure rates — three very different physical behaviours — within a single flexible family.

The distribution is defined by two parameters. The shape parameter k (sometimes written β) controls whether the failure rate increases, decreases, or remains constant over time. When k > 1, the failure rate increases with time — this models wear-out failures typical of mechanical components, where parts degrade with use. When k = 1, the Weibull distribution reduces exactly to the exponential distribution with constant failure rate, modelling purely random failures such as electronic components failing at a steady background rate. When k < 1, the failure rate decreases over time — this models infant-mortality failures, where defective items fail early and the survivors become more reliable. The scale parameter λ (sometimes written η) is the characteristic life: at x = λ, the CDF equals 1 − e⁻¹ ≈ 63.2%, regardless of k.

The probability density function (PDF) f(x) gives the relative likelihood of observing a failure at exactly time x. The cumulative distribution function (CDF) F(x) gives the probability that a component will have failed by time x — this is also called the unreliability. The reliability function R(x) = 1 − F(x) gives the probability of survival past time x, which is the primary metric for warranty and maintenance planning. The hazard rate h(x) = f(x) / R(x) is the instantaneous failure rate at time x given survival up to that point; in engineering it is called the force of mortality or hazard function.

The mean of the Weibull distribution is λ · Γ(1 + 1/k), where Γ is the gamma function. The median is λ · (ln 2)^(1/k). The mode (most likely failure time) is λ · ((k−1)/k)^(1/k) when k > 1 and zero when k ≤ 1. The variance is λ² · [Γ(1 + 2/k) − (Γ(1 + 1/k))²].

Weibull analysis appears in fleet maintenance scheduling, aircraft component certification, wind-energy resource assessment, earthquake-return-period estimation, and cancer survival studies. This calculator performs all standard Weibull computations in a single step, using the Lanczos approximation for the gamma function to maintain high numerical accuracy across a wide range of parameter values.

howto.heading: How to use the Weibull distribution calculator
steps: Enter the shape parameter k — values above 1 model wear-out, k=1 is exponential, values below 1 model infant mortality.
Enter the scale parameter λ, which represents the characteristic life (the time by which about 63.2% of units will have failed).
Enter the value x at which you want to evaluate the distribution — typically a time, distance, or stress level.
Click Calculate to get the PDF, CDF, reliability, hazard rate, mean, median, mode, variance, and standard deviation.

items.q: What does the shape parameter k mean in practice?
items.a: The shape parameter k determines the failure-rate pattern. When k < 1, failure rate decreases over time — early defects dominate. When k = 1, failure rate is constant — purely random failures. When k > 1, failure rate increases — wear-out is the dominant failure mode. Most mechanical components have k between 1 and 4.

items.q: What is the reliability function and how do I use it?
items.a: The reliability R(x) = 1 − F(x) gives the probability that a component survives beyond time x. For planning maintenance schedules or warranty periods, you choose an acceptable failure probability and solve for the corresponding x. For example, R(x) = 0.90 means 90% of units are expected to survive beyond x.

items.q: Why does CDF always equal about 63.2% at x=λ?
items.a: At x = λ, the exponent in the CDF formula becomes (λ/λ)^k = 1, so F(λ) = 1 − e⁻¹ ≈ 0.6321. This is true for any value of k, making λ the universal characteristic life: 63.2% of units will have failed by the scale parameter, regardless of the shape.

items.q: What is the hazard rate and when does it matter?
items.a: The hazard rate h(x) is the instantaneous rate of failure at time x, given survival to that point. In reliability engineering it is used to schedule preventive maintenance. When h(x) is increasing (k > 1), replacing parts before they reach high-hazard age is cost-effective. When h(x) is constant (k = 1), replacement timing does not matter statistically.

items.q: How is the Weibull mean different from the scale parameter?
items.a: The scale parameter λ is the time at which 63.2% of units have failed — it is not the mean lifetime. The mean is λ · Γ(1 + 1/k). For k=1 (exponential), mean = λ. For k=2, mean ≈ 0.886 λ. For k=3.44, mean ≈ λ. So the mean exceeds or falls below λ depending on the shape.

about.heading: About the weighted mean calculator
about.body:
The weighted mean — also called the weighted average — is a generalization of the arithmetic mean that accounts for the fact that not all values contribute equally to the average. Each value is multiplied by a weight that reflects its importance, frequency, or proportion, and the products are summed and then divided by the total weight. When all weights are equal, the weighted mean reduces to the simple arithmetic mean, which is why the arithmetic mean is a special case of the weighted mean.

The formula is x̄w = (w₁x₁ + w₂x₂ + … + wₙxₙ) / (w₁ + w₂ + … + wₙ). The weights can be any positive numbers — they do not need to sum to 1 or to 100. Proportional weights (summing to 1) and percentage weights (summing to 100) give the same result, and so do integer frequency weights. The calculator normalises automatically, so you can enter class sizes, portfolio dollar amounts, or survey response counts directly as weights without converting them to fractions.

Grade calculation is one of the most common uses. A course might assign 20% weight to homework, 30% to the midterm, and 50% to the final. If a student scores 88 on homework, 72 on the midterm, and 84 on the final, the weighted mean is (0.20×88 + 0.30×72 + 0.50×84) / 1.0 = (17.6 + 21.6 + 42.0) = 81.2. A simple average of 88, 72, and 84 would give 81.33 — close but different, and the difference grows when weights are very unequal.

In finance, the weighted mean is used to compute the average return on a portfolio where each asset has a different dollar value invested. An investor with $10,000 in Asset A (returning 5%), $25,000 in Asset B (returning 8%), and $15,000 in Asset C (returning −2%) has a portfolio return of (10000×0.05 + 25000×0.08 + 15000×(−0.02)) / 50000 = (500 + 2000 − 300) / 50000 = 4.4%. The simple average return of 3.67% would be misleading because Asset B represents half the portfolio.

In statistics, the weighted mean arises whenever samples are drawn with unequal probabilities or when sub-group means must be combined: a national average income that properly weights each region by its population, a meta-analysis that weights studies by their sample sizes, or a survey that post-stratifies responses by demographic group. In physics and engineering, the centre of mass is the weighted mean position where the weights are the masses of individual components. The weighted mean is also fundamental to expectation in probability theory: E[X] = Σ xᵢ P(X=xᵢ), which is precisely the weighted mean with probabilities as weights.

items.q: What is the weighted mean?
items.a: The weighted mean is an average where each value contributes proportionally to its assigned weight. It equals the sum of each value multiplied by its weight, divided by the total weight. When all weights are equal, it reduces to the simple arithmetic mean.

items.q: Do the weights need to sum to 1 or 100?
items.a: No. Weights can be any positive numbers. Whether you enter proportions that sum to 1, percentages that sum to 100, or raw counts that sum to any value, the calculator divides by the total weight automatically, so the result is always correct. Negative weights are not permitted.

items.q: How does the weighted mean differ from the arithmetic mean?
items.a: The arithmetic mean treats all values identically. The weighted mean allows some values to count more than others based on their weights. A grade calculator using equal weights gives the same result as the arithmetic mean; a grade calculator with different assignment weights gives the weighted mean, which is almost always the more meaningful result.

items.q: What happens if all weights are the same?
items.a: When all weights are equal — for example, all 1s — the formula simplifies to the sum of the values divided by the number of values, which is the arithmetic mean. You can verify this by entering equal weights for any data set; the weighted mean will match the simple average.

items.q: Can I use the weighted mean for survey data?
items.a: Yes. Enter the response values (e.g., ratings from 1 to 5) as values, and the number of respondents choosing each rating as weights. The result is the mean rating, properly accounting for how many people gave each response. This is also called a frequency-weighted mean.

items.q: What if my values include negative numbers?
items.a: Negative values are fully supported. Common examples include portfolio returns, where some assets lose money, or temperature anomalies that include values below a baseline. The weights, however, must all be positive — a negative weight has no natural interpretation and is rejected by the calculator.
